Data Architect Consultation: Document Database for Historical Change Tracking
Hourly - Expert - Est. Time: More than 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
Data Architect Consultation: Document Database for Historical Change Tracking We are seeking experienced data architects and engineers for a paid discovery engagement. We have accumulated monthly snapshots of a large administrative dataset spanning several years and are exploring approaches for converting these periodic extracts into historical entity records using a document database approach. Our goals include: • Preserving record-level changes over time • Maintaining flexible historical records as source data changes • Identifying meaningful changes in status, attributes, locations, and relationships • Supporting future prediction, experimentation, and alerting workflows At this stage, we are not looking for someone to immediately build the system. Instead, we are looking for practitioners who can review the problem, ask questions, and propose potential architectures, technologies, and implementation approaches. Selected candidates will be invited to a paid consultation where we will share additional details about the dataset, existing analyses, and project objectives. ## Examples Perhaps you have designed systems such as: • A healthcare platform that reconstructs patient histories from periodic exports of electronic health records and preserves changing attributes over time. • A bank or fintech platform that maintains historical customer profiles, detects meaningful changes in account behavior, and flags accounts at risk of fraud, churn, or closure. • A customer data platform that ingests periodic CRM exports and maintains evolving customer profiles, addresses, subscriptions, and engagement histories. • An insurance system that tracks policyholders over multiple years, including coverage lapses, address changes, claims history, and renewal risks. • A logistics or supply chain platform that reconstructs shipment, vendor, or inventory histories and generates alerts when patterns change. • A public sector system that maintains historical records of residents, permits, licenses, benefits recipients, or other administrative records. • A census, demographic, or population research project that follows individuals or households over time to study mobility, participation, or attrition. • A university system that tracks students longitudinally, identifies those at risk of dropping out, and triggers interventions from advisors. If you have worked on systems like these, especially using document databases or flexible schema approaches, we would be interested in hearing how you approached data modeling, identity resolution, historical change tracking, and alert generation. ## To Apply Please tell us: 1. Briefly describe your experience designing document databases or flexible-schema systems that preserve historical changes over time. 2. What industries or domains have you worked in? Examples might include healthcare, finance, insurance, customer data platforms, logistics, higher education, public sector, civic technology, or scientific research. 3. If you were handed monthly snapshots of a large dataset spanning several years, what questions would you ask before proposing an architecture? 4. What technologies would you initially consider and why? Examples might include MongoDB, Couchbase, Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, BigQuery, PostgreSQL JSONB, object storage, dbt, Airflow, Python, or hybrid architectures. 5. Have you designed systems involving any of the following? • Document databases • Flexible or evolving schemas • Identity resolution • Historical record reconstruction • Temporal data modeling • Change Data Capture (CDC) • Event sourcing • Master Data Management (MDM) • Alerting systems ## Engagement Structure We expect to speak with several candidates, compensate them for their time, and then determine whether to engage an individual, firm, or team for a larger implementation effort. Compensation for the discovery consultation will be discussed with selected candidates.

Proposals: Fewer than 5
Fractional CTO & Principal Solution Architect
Fixed-price - Intermediate - Est. Budget: $300
We are seeking a highly experienced, bilingual (Business + Tech) Fractional CTO and Principal Solution Architect to review our comprehensive Product Requirements Document (PRD) , conduct a rigorous gap analysis, consult on commercial optimization, and deliver a production-ready technical architecture specification document (.docx). You will bridge the gap between business strategy and deep software engineering, ensuring our platform is scalable, secure, and structurally sound before development begins. --- 1. Project Overview & Context The project is the BotsFuture Learning Platform - an avatar-led, project-focused educational ecosystem that teaches physical computing and programming to kids ages 8 to 11. Core Product Mechanics The Guided 6-Phase Flow: Every project drives the student through a fixed sequence: Introduction, Teaching/Concept Quiz, Physical Assembly/Wiring, Block Coding, Upload & Test, and a Final Challenge. The Visual IDE Component: Built using Blockly.js with the Zelos renderer to create a Scratch-style interface. The blocks generate Arduino C++ code. Hardware Execution Pipeline: The browser compiles the C++ program via a server-side toolchain and flashes it to hardware (Arduino Uno, Nano, ESP32) using the browser's native Web Serial API. Dual-Deployment Topology: The app operates from a single codebase but handles two distinct deployment shapes: 1. A multi-tenant, cloud-hosted Public Edition with an integrated hardware marketplace. 2. An entirely offline, local network-hosted School Edition that runs on a school LAN with zero internet connectivity, utilizing localized AI and local text-to-speech (TTS) engines. --- 2. Two-Phased Scope of Work Phase 1: Gap Analysis & Business-Technology Consultation Before designing the architecture, you will collaborate directly with our leadership team as a strategic consultant to achieve the following: Identify Structural Gaps: Audit our clear product vision to uncover hidden technical roadblocks, logical inconsistencies, or operational friction within the dual-hosting requirements. Revenue & Success Maximization: Review cross-cutting features—such as the programmatic integration with our third-party hardware vendor , user onboarding, and monetization frameworks—and provide expert consulting on how to architect these components to maximize platform adoption and revenue. Unified Strategic Alignment: Lead short consulting sessions with us to evaluate trade-offs, solve open architectural questions (e.g., telemetry data retention, COPPA/GDPR compliance paths for public sign-ups), and align on a unanimous strategic direction. -- Phase 2: Complete Architectural Design & SDLC Documentation Once strategic decisions are finalized, you will transition into a core Solution Architect role to dissect the project into fine-grained engineering modules. You will generate a master Technical Architecture Specification Document (.docx) containing: 1. Exact Feature Inventory: A definitive, granular breakdown of MVP boundaries versus future roadmap items (e.g., circuit simulation, tablet companion apps). 2. User Roles & Cryptographic System Claims: Absolute enforcement matrices isolating Student, Teacher, and BotsFuture Admin capabilities. 3. Database Structure & Relational Schema: Production-ready relational design (e.g., Prisma/PostgreSQL definitions) including exact entities for sessions, historical rollbacks, and manifest version pinning. 4. Complete Screen/Module Dissection: Detailed architectural mappings of the 3-Panel IDE layout, the low-code Manifest Authoring Studio, and the Teacher Analytics Console. 5. Deterministic AI Pipeline & Tech Stack: Structural layouts defining the rule-led Avatar Orchestrator, its handoffs to quantized localized Language Models (e.g., Ollama/Llama), and TTS abstraction layers. 6. SDLC Diagrams & Artifacts: Technical system topologies, end-to-end data pipelines, browser-to-hardware serialization sequences, and state machine transitions. 7. Development Milestones & Sprints: A realistic 28-week phased execution plan mapping milestones, testing strategies, and QA validation criteria. 8. Commercial Cost Estimates: Accurate resource allocation models and budgetary projections categorized by development agency vs. senior freelancer strategies. --- 3. Candidate Experience & Profile Requirements Proven CTO / Solution Architect Background: Demonstrable experience taking complex software platforms from 0-to-1 production launch. Hybrid Infrastructure Architecture Expertise: Deep experience designing software capable of switching dynamically between cloud multi-tenancy and entirely network-isolated, on-premise local server environments. Physical Computing / Web-to-Hardware Familiarity: Highly preferred experience dealing with visual programming blocks (Blockly/Scratch frameworks) , browser-based terminal/serial communications (Web Serial API) , or hardware compiler toolchains. Data Privacy Champion: Solid understanding of technical compliance requirements for minors, including data minimization and isolated storage structures. Exceptional Documentarian: A history of translating product requirements into highly structured technical specifications with scannable, developer-ready clarity.

Claude Code & Prompt Engineering Expert — multi-agent AI systems
Hourly: $40-$70 - Expert - Est. Time: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
We run a production multi-agent AI system: a fleet of agents that do real marketing work for real clients (research, creative, social, paid-media analysis, presentations). The platform agents run on GPT-5-class models; internally we build, orchestrate and automate heavily with Claude Code (skills, subagents, MCP servers, hooks, gates). It's live and used daily, so quality and reliability are everything. THE ROLE You'll own AI quality and agent engineering across both worlds. Day to day: - Prompt engineering & design: write, refactor and optimise agent prompts so they behave reliably — call the right tool at the right moment, follow the workflow, hit the gates, and never leak internal/technical detail to the end user. This is craft, and you're expert at it. - Claude Code engineering: build and improve agentic workflows in Claude Code — authoring and hardening skills, wiring MCP tools, designing subagent orchestration, hooks, and gates (deterministic checks that stop an agent before it does the wrong thing). You know this environment cold. - Behaviour debugging: when an agent hallucinates, repeats itself, picks the wrong tool, or surfaces something it shouldn't, you read the logs and execution traces, find the real root cause, and fix it at the prompt / tool / gate level. - Evals & regression testing: build and maintain evals so a change that fixes one thing doesn't quietly break another. Measure before/after with evidence, not vibes. - Tool / MCP wiring: slot new data sources and tools into agents and tune prompts so they use them efficiently (fewer wasted or failed tool calls). WHO YOU ARE This is a senior, high-ownership role. "Can write a prompt" is not enough — we need someone who owns outcomes. - Absolute Claude Code expert. You live in it: skills, subagents, MCP, hooks, gates, agentic orchestration. You've built real, reliable systems with it, not toy demos. - Expert prompt engineer with genuine craft and a strong mental model of why agents behave the way they do — tool-use, multi-step workflows, context, gating. - Deep understanding of AI models. You know the current Claude and GPT families, their strengths, failure modes, and how to get the best out of each. You pick the right model and reasoning settings for the job. - You judge the output, not the prompt. If a result looks wrong, it's wrong — whatever the instructions say. You trust your eye, push back, and take ownership of quality. We don't want someone who follows the prompt literally and ships a bad result. - High initiative, low hand-holding. You spot problems before we flag them, dig to the real root cause, and prove your work moved the needle. - Comfortable reading logs, traces and SQL (Postgres/Supabase). Python for scripting, evals and glue. NICE TO HAVE - Background in marketing / advertising / creative tooling. - Experience with eval frameworks (OpenAI Evals or similar). - Image / video generation pipelines and routing. HOW WE WORK Async-friendly and fast-moving, with some UK-hours overlap helpful. We use Linear for tickets and GitHub for code, and we value plain-English communication — explain things simply, no jargon walls. TO APPLY In your proposal, tell us briefly about: 1) A real agentic system you built or fixed in Claude Code (skills / MCP / subagents / gates) and what made it reliable. 2) A time you diagnosed a misbehaving LLM agent from logs or traces, and how you proved your fix actually worked. 3) How you decide which AI model and settings to use for a task, and how you design gates to stop an agent doing the wrong thing. A short Loom or a tight paragraph is perfect — we care about how you think, not cover-letter polish.

Hourly: $10-$150 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, 30+ hrs/week
We're an AI infrastructure company building a Voice AI receptionist platform for medical and dental practices — an AI that answers patient calls, qualifies them, books appointments, and follows up 24/7. The marketing and demand side is already strong; we have practices waiting. Our priority now is building the real application and getting the security + HIPAA compliance right the first time, at scale. We're looking for a developer (or small team) who can wear two hats: ship the application and architect it to be HIPAA-compliant from the ground up. You'll work directly with the founder and our technical lead. WHAT YOU'LL BE BUILDING 1. A custom dashboard / web app (currently bare-bones) — practice-facing portal for call review, lead pipeline, and appointment/booking management. Front end + API + database. 2. Voice AI agents on Retell — configuring, deploying, and iterating inbound/outbound voice agents (knowledge bases, call flows, transfers, post-call data capture). Retell, Vapi, or similar experience strongly preferred. 3. The HIPAA / PHI compliance layer — the hard part and the reason for this hire (below). THE HIPAA / COMPLIANCE WORK (MOST IMPORTANT) Patient data (PHI) is moving off our voice vendor and into our own application. We need someone who can own the technical half of HIPAA: - Encryption in transit and at rest, secure architecture, least-privilege access, MFA, and tamper-proof audit logging. - PHI de-identification before LLM calls — masking real patient data before it hits OpenAI/Anthropic and re-inserting on return, with minimal added latency (latency matters a lot for live voice). - Patient-rights mechanisms — working "right to erasure" / deletion that propagates to every system the data touched, plus right-to-access and an append-only consent ledger (exact wording, version, timestamp, response). - Retention / auto-purge policies and BAA/DPA handling with sub-processors. - Helping us evaluate and integrate a PHI vault / proxy (tokenization vault where flagged PHI never sits in our own DB) vs. building controls ourselves. We understand HIPAA is self-attested and roughly half technical, half policy/process. You own the technical half and help us document due diligence to stay audit-ready. OUR CURRENT STACK - Voice: Retell (Anthropic Claude for reasoning) — BAA-capable - Frontend / hosting: Next.js on Vercel - Database / backend: Supabase (Postgres) - Automation: Make.com - Other: Twilio (numbers/SMS), Stripe (billing) You don't need every one — tell us which you've shipped production work with. YOU'RE A STRONG FIT IF YOU HAVE - Proven full-stack delivery (TypeScript/Node, React/Next.js, Postgres) on production SaaS. - Real HIPAA experience in a US healthcare product — not theoretical. Bonus: HITECH, SOC 2, PHIPA/PIPEDA, GDPR. - Experience with voice AI (Retell/Vapi/LiveKit) or conversational AI agents. - Experience integrating LLM APIs (Anthropic/OpenAI) safely, including de-identification patterns. - Bonus: dental/medical practice software (PMS) integration (Open Dental, Dentrix, etc.). - Clear communicator who flags risk and tradeoffs early. We move fast but we will not cut corners on PHI. ENGAGEMENT & BUDGET - Hourly, ongoing engagement. We have not fixed a rate — we want your honest quote. - Range posted is wide because the roles differ (full-stack build vs. HIPAA/security architecture). Quote the rate that reflects your real expertise — we pay for people who get PHI right. - We'll begin with a small paid trial milestone (about $300–$500) before any larger commitment. - Overlap with North American (Mountain Time) hours preferred. Start immediately. IN YOUR PROPOSAL, PLEASE INCLUDE 1. Your hourly rate. 2. A rough estimate (hours + cost) to take our app from bare-bones to a compliant, launchable MVP — a ballpark range is fine. 3. A healthcare/HIPAA project you shipped — your specific role and how you handled PHI. 4. Whether you've worked with Retell or another voice-AI platform (link a build if you can). 5. Your read, in 1–2 sentences, on the best way to handle PHI de-identification for a low-latency voice app. 6. What you'd charge for the trial task: "Design the PHI de-identification flow + audit-log schema for a low-latency Retell voice app (written doc + diagram)." Applications that skip items 5 and 6 will not be reviewed. Required 1. Describe a HIPAA-compliant system you personally built and your specific role in it. 2. In 1–2 sentences: best approach to PHI de-identification for a low-latency voice app? 3. What is your hourly rate, and your ballpark estimate (hours + cost) for the MVP?

One is a Run-Client-Script action that POSTs to our REST op and shows the result in a toast or modal. Role-gate both. Capture all sys_ux_* and declarative-action records into the application scope so they version with the app, and confirm each record's Application field is set to our scoped app. What we provide A detailed implementation runbook for every step above. Fixed-price - Expert - Est. Budget: $700
We're looking for a senior AI engineer to build a production-grade RAG system with AI agents that makes our document archive queryable with accurate, source-cited answers. The work involves building a retrieval-augmented generation pipeline over our PDFs and scanned documents. We need OCR-based ingestion with structure-preserving extraction, hybrid retrieval with reranking over a vector database, and a conversational AI chatbot that answers questions with citations back to the exact source document. On top of retrieval, we need an AI agent layer that validates extracted fields, flags missing data and discrepancies, and routes uncertain cases to human review instead of guessing. Accuracy is non-negotiable, the system must never fabricate numbers, codes, or figures, so every answer has to be grounded in retrieved content with anti-hallucination safeguards throughout. What we need built: •⁠ ⁠Document ingestion pipeline with OCR fallback for scanned files •⁠ ⁠Structure-preserving extraction and chunking with metadata •⁠ ⁠Embeddings into a vector database •⁠ ⁠Hybrid (semantic + keyword) retrieval with a reranking layer •⁠ ⁠A multi-agent workflow (LangChain / LangGraph) for validation, discrepancy detection, and human-in-the-loop review •⁠ ⁠A source-cited chat interface where every answer references its source Ideal candidate has shipped accuracy-critical RAG and document-intelligence systems in production, with strong command of Python, LangChain, AI agent development, vector databases, and OCR pipelines. Clean, documented code and clear communication matter to us. This is a one time project but might turn into a long term contract if the situation allows. Please share relevant RAG / document-intelligence / AI agent work when you apply. Also share your availability hours each week.

Proposals: 50+
I-Powered Search + RAG Answer System for a Legal Education Platform
Hourly: $75-$150 - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Overview We run a continuing legal education (CLE) platform with a large catalog of courses (live, replay, and on-demand) across dozens of practice areas, plus full session transcripts and video. We're building an AI-powered search experience and need a developer to build the system behind it. The UI is already designed, and a written spec for the ranking logic is finalized — we'll share both with shortlisted candidates. We want a working v1 live quickly, then iterate. There are two connected parts to this build. Part 1 — AI Catalog Search (with custom ranking logic) Natural-language search over our course catalog that returns relevant classes and applies a specific, already-defined ranking and grouping logic. Semantic search over the catalog, filterable by practice area, topic, credit type, and jurisdiction. A ranking + fill engine implemented exactly to our spec (you implement it; you don't design it): Format tiers: Live + Replay are one combined tier and always rank above On-demand. Sort within tiers: Live/Replay by date (nearest upcoming first); On-demand by popularity (we supply the popularity list). Fill: show the total match count (e.g., "80 classes match"), render 10 — walk Live/Replay by date first, top up with most-popular On-demand, then link to the full set ("See all 80"). Grouping: 3 or fewer results = flat list; more than 3 = group by format tier, then sub-practice area. A clean, documented API the existing React front end calls. Part 2 — RAG Answer System (transcript + video) A retrieval-augmented search across our session transcripts and video that returns a single, cited answer assembled from four linked panels — all pointing to the same moment in a live CLE: AI Reasoning — a synthesized, plain-language answer grounded strictly in retrieved content (no claim without a source). Transcript — the exact transcript excerpt the answer is drawn from, with speaker and timestamp. Video — the live-session clip, deep-linked to that same timestamp. Speaker Information — the faculty member who spoke the passage, with bio/credentials. The engineering core is keeping all four panels in sync: every panel keys off the same program + speaker + timestamp anchor, so chunking must preserve speaker and timestamp at the segment level. Stack We use the Anthropic Claude API and are open to your recommendation on vector store and embeddings (pgvector, Pinecone, Qdrant, etc.). Use whatever ships a clean v1 fastest. Required Production experience building semantic / AI search and RAG systems. Strong with vector databases, embeddings, and LLM APIs. Can process long-form transcripts (chunking that preserves speaker + timestamp metadata). Can implement precise ranking/sorting/grouping rules from a written spec. Can build clean, documented APIs for a React front end. Citations that trace back to real sources — minimal hallucination. Nice to have Legal or other accuracy-critical content experience. Video deep-linking / timestamp syncing. n8n, WooCommerce/Stripe familiarity. Engagement Type: [Hourly / Fixed-price — choose one] Timeline: Start now, working v1 live quickly. Catalog search can ship first; RAG answer system can follow. Budget: [Insert range, or "propose based on scope"] To apply, briefly tell us A RAG or AI search system you've built, and the corpus size. How you'd get a basic working version live fast. Your suggested vector store, embedding model, and LLM. How you'd keep the four answer panels (reasoning, transcript, video, speaker) pointing to the same source moment.

Proposals: 20 to 50
LLM / RAG Architecture Consultant — Review & Advise on-Prem Local AI Build
Hourly - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Less than 30 hrs/week
We're building an internal AI system that runs entirely on our own hardware (no cloud inference) against our own company data. We have a working proof-of-concept and want to get the architecture right. We need an experienced consultant to review what we've built, pressure-test our decisions, and tell us where we're wrong. This is an advisory/validation role first — we have someone doing the hands-on work; what we want is a senior second opinion to make sure we're building this the right way. What we're running today: Inference: RTX 5090 (32GB, Blackwell), Ubuntu 24.04, running llama-server (llama.cpp + CUDA) serving Gemma 4 31B-it (Q4_K_M GGUF) at a 262,144 context window. Also hosts our MCP retrieval server, PostgreSQL, and Qdrant. Embeddings: separate machine with an RTX 3060 running vLLM serving Qwen3-Embedding-4B. RAG: hybrid retrieval — Postgres full-text search + Qdrant semantic search with RRF fusion, exposed through a custom MCP server with tool-calling. Data: ingesting our own internal operational data into Postgres + Qdrant. Planned stack: LiteLLM for model routing, n8n for automation, Open WebUI for the interface, Langfuse for observability, Vault or Infisical for secrets, Keycloak/Azure AD for SSO. What we need help with: Validating our two-machine split (inference vs. embeddings) and whether our VRAM/context budget holds up under real load — specifically whether a 256K context window is real and performant on a single 32GB card or just nominal. Model selection and routing strategy: which open-weight models for which tasks, and how to structure LiteLLM routes. RAG quality: chunking, embedding dimensionality, hybrid search tuning, reranking — making retrieval actually accurate on messy real-world data. Sanity-checking our overall architecture and telling us our blind spots. You should have done: Stood up local LLM inference in production — llama.cpp/llama-server and vLLM, not just Ollama on a laptop. You understand GGUF quantization (Q4_K_M, IQ-series), KV cache, KV-cache quantization, and how context length maps to actual VRAM consumption. Real fluency in GPU sizing math — given a model, a quant, and a context window, you can tell us whether it fits on a given card and what throughput to expect. Bonus if you've worked with Blackwell / sm_120a. Built production RAG — vector DBs (Qdrant, pgvector), hybrid search, RRF fusion, embedding model selection, reranking, evaluation. Worked with agentic/tool-calling systems and ideally MCP servers. Know the open-weight model landscape (Gemma, Qwen, Llama, Mistral, Phi, Nemotron, Hermes) and their licenses well enough to advise. Production ops: systemd, Docker, model gateways (LiteLLM or similar), observability (Langfuse), secrets management, SSO.

Senior Supabase / TypeScript / AI Pipeline Engineer
Hourly: $70-$85 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Hours to be determined
UPWORK POST — Senior Supabase / TypeScript / AI Pipeline Engineer Needed to Fix Launch-Blocking Data Extraction Issue We are looking for a senior software engineer to help resolve a launch-blocking issue in a consumer-facing web platform that analyzes public online source material and turns it into structured, user-facing intelligence. This is not a simple website styling job. We need someone who can debug a real backend/data pipeline issue involving source retrieval, classification/entity matching, Supabase Edge Functions, TypeScript, OpenAI API calls, structured JSON extraction, database writes, and frontend presentation. The platform is already built and close to launch, but we are stuck on a specific quality/density problem in the backend pipeline. The current bottleneck: The system retrieves public source material, classifies/matches the user’s search entity, filters sources, sends evidence into an AI extraction step, and writes structured outcomes to Supabase. The problem is that valid, specific signals exist in the raw source data, but too many of those signals are getting lost, compressed, merged, or weakened before they appear in the final structured output. We need an engineer who can trace the full path: raw source data → entity/classification matching → source filtering → buyer/user signal validation → AI extraction payload → AI JSON response → post-processing → Supabase insert → frontend display The engineer must determine exactly where the data loss/compression is happening and implement a durable fix, not a temporary prompt Band-Aid. Required skills: * Strong TypeScript * Supabase * Supabase Edge Functions * PostgreSQL / SQL * Deno or serverless TypeScript runtime experience * OpenAI API / structured JSON extraction experience * Debugging AI-assisted data pipelines * Prompt-to-schema extraction workflows * Source filtering / entity matching / classification logic * React frontend familiarity * Ability to work carefully in an existing production-adjacent codebase * Ability to diagnose without breaking existing functionality Strongly preferred: * Experience with retrieval + classification + extraction pipelines * Experience with schema-constrained AI outputs * Experience debugging cases where AI output summarizes instead of preserving detailed source-level evidence * Experience with Supabase logs, SQL validation, Edge Function testing, and structured diagnostics * Experience building mobile-friendly web apps or Progressive Web Apps Mobile requirement: This is a website, but most users need to use it easily from their phones. We do not want to build a full native iOS/Android app that must go through the Apple App Store or Google Play Store. We need guidance and implementation support for a mobile-first web experience, likely including responsive design and/or Progressive Web App behavior, so users can access the platform from their phones without typing the website address every time. Important working requirement: We need this solved in no more than one week. Please do not apply if your approach is only “rewrite the prompt” or “add another AI instruction.” We need someone who can inspect the actual data path and prove where the loss occurs. What we will provide: * Existing Supabase project structure * Edge Function code * Relevant tables * Example test case * Current diagnostics/logs * Known failure pattern * Step-by-step reproduction process * Access as appropriate after NDA or confidentiality agreement Ideal deliverables: 1. Confirm the exact root cause of the density/compression issue. 2. Show evidence of where source-level data is being lost or merged. 3. Implement a durable fix. 4. Validate the fix with controlled test cases. 5. Preserve source quality while improving output density. 6. Avoid product-specific hardcoding. 7. Document what changed and why. 8. Advise on mobile-first/PWA deployment path. Please include in your response: * Your experience with Supabase Edge Functions and TypeScript. * Your experience with OpenAI structured JSON extraction. * A brief explanation of how you would debug this pipeline. * Whether you have built or improved a mobile-first web/PWA experience. * Your availability this week. * Whether you can commit to solving or clearly diagnosing the issue within 7 days. We are looking for someone careful, senior, and practical. This project needs root-cause debugging, not guesswork.

AI-Powered HR Manager - Build It, Automate It, Hand It Off
Hourly: $20-$40 - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Hours to be determined
AI-Powered HR Manager: Build It, Automate It, Hand It Off ----------------------------------------------------------------------- WHAT THIS IS We're a multi-location retail business and we need one sharp HR person to build our entire people operation from scratch and run it lean using AI. You'll get access to a Claude team account and the tools you need to move fast. In return, we expect you to work smarter than a traditional HR person, not harder. The hours are lower because the tools are better. The output standard is high because you'll have everything you need to meet it. Job descriptions, some policies, and KPI frameworks are already partially built. Your job is to take what exists, sharpen it, fill the gaps, automate everything you can, and deliver a system that runs with minimal intervention from anyone. We are investing in the right person and the right tools. We expect 10x the value of a traditional HR hire in half the hours. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- WHAT WE'RE GIVING YOU - Claude team account: use it for drafting, research, screening, template generation, policy writing, everything - Existing job descriptions, partial policies, and KPI frameworks to build on. You are not starting from zero - Direct owner access for fast decisions, no bureaucracy, no approval chains - A custom internal HR portal where your content lives and gets automated going forward - Clear scope, clear milestones, and clear payment triggers with no ambiguity about what done looks like ----------------------------------------------------------------------- THE ENGAGEMENT: TWO PHASES PHASE 1 - Build the Foundation + Make 5 Hires 4-6 weeks | Project-based | Milestone payments - Audit all existing job descriptions, policies, and salary ranges: benchmark against current market data, flag gaps, and finalize with owner before any offer goes out - Build and complete KPI frameworks for every role (managers and associates) so every hire knows exactly what success looks like from Day 1 - Post, source, screen, and close 5 management hires across our 3 locations (see below) - Build full HR infrastructure: employee handbook, onboarding checklists per role, offer letter templates, and compliance docs, all using AI where possible - Set up ATS with automated screening, self-book interview scheduling, and AI-assisted candidate filtering - Migrate payroll to Gusto and/or Paychex, live and running before Phase 2 starts - Feed all content into our internal HR portal so the AI layer can maintain and automate it going forward - Deliver a clean handoff guide so routine HR runs without you PHASE 2 - Run HR + Payroll Ongoing 2-4 hrs/week | Ongoing Upwork contract | Paid weekly or bi-weekly - Process weekly payroll across all locations: accurate, on time, every time, automated as far as the platform allows - Handle ongoing hiring as new roles open, staggered, not all at once - Own all employee HR questions: the owner is never the first call - Keep compliance current: local labor law updates annually and you stay ahead of them - Continuously improve and automate, always reducing manual work over time - Update HR portal content as policies and roles evolve ----------------------------------------------------------------------- PHASE 1 MILESTONES & PAYMENT SCHEDULE All Phase 1 payments are milestone-triggered. No milestone, no payment. M1 - Compensation and KPI audit complete across all roles, owner sign-off received M2 - All 5 manager job posts live, ATS configured, active pipeline running M3 - All 5 manager offers accepted, start dates set M4 - Full HR infrastructure delivered: handbook, onboarding, templates, compliance, portal content M5 - Payroll migrated, first clean payroll run complete in Gusto/Paychex Phase 2: Ongoing HR and payroll, 8-12 hrs/week ----------------------------------------------------------------------- PHASE 1 HIRING SCOPE: 5 ROLES Managers come first. Associates are hired in waves underneath them. You manage the sequencing so payroll ramps up at a pace that makes sense. Role Store Manager Assistant Store Manager Laundromat Manager (24/7 operation) Compensation for all roles is competitive. Details are available during the interview process. Your M1 deliverable includes validating and finalizing all comp ranges before any offer goes out. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- KPIs ARE A CORE DELIVERABLE Before the first hire is made, you will build out KPI frameworks for every role. We have some frameworks started. Your job is to complete them, make them measurable, and make sure every person we hire knows exactly what success looks like from Day 1. - Store managers: sales targets, shrink rate, labor cost percentage, customer satisfaction, team retention, etc - Assistant managers: shift performance, task completion, team development metrics - Location manager (24/7): uptime, cleanliness scores, incident reports, revenue per shift - Associates: productivity targets, attendance, cross-training progress These KPIs feed into our internal system and become how we manage and evaluate performance going forward. Build them right the first time and you will be responsible fully yo track these. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- HOW WE EXPECT YOU TO USE AI You'll have a Claude team account from Day 1. We expect you to use it actively. The reason the hours for this role are lean is because AI handles a significant portion of the drafting, research, and routine work. That is the deal. - Use Claude to draft job descriptions, policies, onboarding docs, offer letters, and KPI frameworks - Use AI screening tools or Claude to filter applications before you spend time on them - Use AI to research compliance updates, benchmark salaries, and generate first drafts of any HR document - Use automation wherever the payroll platform or ATS allows and document what you've automated so it stays automated - Feed everything into our internal HR portal so the AI layer can maintain it going forward If you are not comfortable using AI as a core part of your workflow, this role is not for you. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR Must Have: - 3+ years HR experience in retail, hourly, or multi-location environments - Full-cycle recruiting: you have sourced, screened, and closed management and hourly roles end to end - Payroll management experience: you have run payroll, not just supported someone who did - Hands-on Gusto and/or Paychex experience - Genuine comfort with AI tools: you use them daily and you are not learning on the job here - Knowledge of local labor law in your operating jurisdiction, including wage ordinances, paid leave requirements, and scheduling laws - Ability to build HR infrastructure from scratch and hand it off clean Strong Bonus: - Experience migrating between payroll platforms - Has built KPI frameworks for hourly retail or service roles - Multi-location or franchise HR background - Experience building automated HR systems that reduced manual work measurably ----------------------------------------------------------------------- CONTRACT TERMS This is an Upwork contract. Here is exactly what that means: Contract Type : Upwork hourly contract, all work tracked and paid through Upwork Phase 1 : Project-based with milestone payments (see above), rate set at contract start Phase 2 : Ongoing hourly contract, 8-12 hrs/week, paid weekly or bi-weekly through Upwork Communication : Weekly check-in with owner, more frequent during Phase 1. Available for any additional meetings or calls the owner requests. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- AI TOOLS AND CONFIDENTIALITY POLICY This is a strict requirement, not a suggestion. By accepting this contract you agree to the following: AI Tools You May Use: - You will use only AI tools we provide or approve in writing. This currently means Claude via our team account, and any other tool we explicitly authorize. - You may not use personal AI accounts, free-tier tools, or any external AI platform to process, draft, or analyze any of our HR data, employee information, or business content. - All AI usage must happen within our provided accounts and systems so data stays within our environment at all times. - If you want to use a tool not on our approved list, you ask first. No exceptions. Confidentiality: - Everything you access, create, or learn in this role is strictly confidential: employee records, compensation data, business operations, HR policies, KPIs, candidate information, and all other business information. - You may not share, export, copy, or use any of our data, documents, or systems outside the scope of this contract and our approved tools. - All work product created under this contract belongs to us. You retain no rights to any documents, templates, systems, or processes you build. - You may not use our business information, processes, or data to benefit any other client, employer, or personal project, during or after this engagement. - Upon contract end, all access is revoked immediately. Any copies of our data or documents in your possession must be deleted and confirmed in writing. Violation of any of the above is grounds for immediate contract termination and may result in legal action. This is standard for any serious HR engagement. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- HOW TO APPLY In your Upwork proposal, answer these three questions. Keep each answer to 3-5 sentences. We are looking for substance, not length. 1. Tell us about one HR system you built from scratch. What did it include, how did you use AI in building it, and what does it look like today? 2. How have you used AI tools specifically in HR or payroll work? Give one concrete example with a real outcome. 3. Describe a payroll migration or complex payroll situation you owned end to end. What platform, how many employees, and what was hard about it? Proposals without clear answers to all three will not be reviewed. Generic cover letters will be skipped. ----------------------------------------------------------------------- We are an equal opportunity employer. This is an independent contractor engagement via Upwork. All applicable local, state, and federal labor law compliance is a core responsibility of this role.

Build Multi-User Web Platform with Anthropic AI Agents
Hourly: $25-$50 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
We are turning an internal, single-user AI workflow built in Claude Cowork + Supabase into a secure, multi-user web platform for a B2B trading business. The "intelligence" already exists as a working set of Anthropic Agent Skills, system prompts, and codified business rules. We need an expert team to transition it and build the full platform around it — frontend, backend, and the secure integration with our AI agents. We are not asking you to reinvent the AI logic. We will hand you our skills, prompts, and rules as the spec. Your job is to wrap them in a real product that colleagues can log into and use. We will keep working and fine tuning the agent skills and prompts on our end via Claude. This is an architecture-and-security job as much as a build job. We want someone who has shipped production agentic systems with real multi-user access control and already experienced this transition. What the platform needs to do Colleagues log into a web app and can: Upload a wish list / target list and get an AI-generated offer back. Browse the product catalog and target prices by product. Ask questions and run analyses conversationally ("what can we offer this buyer", "find me products under target price X"), powered by the same AI agents. All of the above with strict, role-based permissions — users only see and do what their role allows. An admin (us) separately maintains the agents — tuning rules, editing prompts, adding skills — outside the web app - via Claude. We will also keep expanding the database in Supabase via Claude. Normal web users must never be able to edit prompts, skills, or business rules. In scope (what you build) 1. Frontend web app Built on Lovable, or a similar rapid app-builder / modern web stack (open to your recommendation — tell us what you'd use and why). Login, role-based UI, wish-list upload, catalog and target-price browsing, and a conversational/chat surface for AI queries. Clean, responsive, business-grade UX. 2. Backend & AI agent integration Server-side integration with Anthropic AI agents (architecture open — propose your approach: hosted agent runtime vs. self-managed agent loop, etc.). Secure handling of API keys and credentials — these must live server-side only, never in the browser. Async job handling for longer agent runs (submit → run → generate output file, e.g. Excel offer → store → notify the user), plus a good streaming/real-time experience in the chat. Supabase is the DB layer. 3. Role-based access control (security-critical) Per-user authorization on every data access and action; the AI agent must never hold broad/admin-level credentials in the multi-user product. Each agent session scoped to the authenticated user's identity and role. Defense-in-depth at the data layer (e.g. row-level security or equivalent), not just in the UI. 4. Admin authoring & deploy workflow Our agents' skills, prompts, and rules stay in a version-controlled source of truth that we maintain. A clean way to publish updates to the live agents, with staging → production promotion and rollback. Web users have no path to edit any of this. Phase 2 (separate milestone — quote separately) 5. Email AI coworker — an inbound address that maps sender → known user/role, runs the same agents/skills on the email + attachments, and returns a draft offer for human approval before sending. 6. WhatsApp AI coworker — a WhatsApp adapter using the same agents; chat thread maps to a persistent session, same identity/authorization rules as the web app. Out of scope (we handle these) Designing the matching/pricing logic (we provide it as skills + rules). Product/business decisions about agent behavior. Frontend design Hard requirements (non-negotiable) API keys and admin credentials never reach the browser. No admin-scoped data access exposed to the agent in the multi-user product — all access is authorization-mediated. Every agent session is scoped to the authenticated user's role. Outbound communications (offers via email/WhatsApp, Phase 2) go through a human-approval step. Architecture choices documented and justified, with a clean separation between frontend, backend/gateway, and agents. Tech you should know Anthropic Claude API / agents / Agent Skills, building and securing multi-tenant web apps, role-based access control and row-level security, async jobs and real-time/streaming UX, rapid app builders (Lovable or similar) and/or modern web stacks. Phase 2: WhatsApp Business / Twilio, inbound email parsing. Deliverables A working, role-based multi-user web app (frontend + backend). Secure AI-agent integration with scoped, per-user access. Admin publish/deploy workflow for agent skills and prompts (staging/prod, rollback). Tests for the authorization layer and a basic eval harness for the AI matching. Handover docs + a short walkthrough.

Proposals: 50+
Expert Workflow & Automation Consultant
Fixed-price - Expert - Est. Budget: $6K
1. Project Overview We operate a high-volume residential home cleaning franchise based in National City, CA. We currently run 6 to 8 active field teams (15 employees) but require a standalone system engineered to dynamically scale up to 12 active field teams and an unlimited company Electric Vehicle (EV) fleet to support future growth. Our corporate CRM (Salesforce) has zero open API access, uses a complex work order accounting ledger, and utilizes a static calendar interface that we are fully abandoning for everyday dispatching operations. Our main operational goal is to maximize automation and completely eliminate manual office friction, chaotic morning staff gossip windows, time theft via personal errands, paper check sheets, evening admin desk bottlenecks, and manual data-entry logs. This system will leverage specialized field software, custom forms, data engines, public data APIs, conversational voice platforms, browser macro bots, and AI to fully automate our fleet dispatching, real-time GPS tracking, automated disciplinary workflows, automated supply/laundry auditing, payroll processing, state labor compliance, anti-undercharging audits, self-service client portal, anti-price manipulation quoting, automated bilingual call routing, training management, recruitment pipeline auditing, and content distribution pipelines. --- 2. Scope of Work 📍 Part A: Custom Variable Quoting, Staggered Dispatching, & Fleet Assignment (Scalable to 12 Teams) * Custom Frontend Quoting Form Build: Replicate our franchise Merry Maids (MM) pricing matrix and variables inside a flexible, standalone frontend form builder embedded on our local website. The custom form must match corporate pricing outcomes exactly to maintain franchise compliance, while serving as the open data gateway for our real estate API public record square footage lookups and automated booking triggers. * Rotational Deep Clean vs. Full Initial Deep Clean Logic: Program a dynamic service model selection module supporting a Gradual Deep Clean Track (gradual, rotational deep clean program over the client's first 4 visits) and a Full Upfront Deep Clean Add-On (applying a heavy upfront multiplier block to visit #1 if the client demands an instantaneous deep clean at launch). * Specialized Project Multipliers: Integrate distinct pricing buttons and operational duration scaling for Move-In/Move-Out cleanings and Post-Construction cleanings within the quoting tool, dynamically adjusting labor parameters and pricing tiers automatically upon selection. * Public Record Square Footage Fetch: Integrate the custom quoting tool with a real estate data API (such as Zillow, Estated, or RentCast) via Zapier or Make.com to automatically pull and lock official home square footages upon address entry to eliminate price manipulation. * Variable Duration Scheduling Engine: Replace rigid legacy fixed slots with a custom backend duration matrix. Job time blocks must automatically calculate using dynamic baseline multipliers based on cleaning frequency, total resident counts, specific room selections, and pet quantities/types. * Demographic & Behavioral Timing Adjustments: Build variable logic rules within the database that adjust job lengths based on household demographics. The system must add time penalties for properties with young children and apply a reduction coefficient for properties with elderly residents to account for localized mess profiles and confined room movement. * Blind Operational Timings: Ensure all backend job duration estimations remain completely hidden from the client-facing interfaces; the customer portal must only display final arrival windows. * Routing Anchors & Constraints: Configure the routing logic to honor strict customer preferences, including designated team requirements (e.g., "Only Sofia") and locked calendar arrivals (e.g., "Mondays at 9:00 AM"). * Dynamic Demand Pricing: Build a variables engine inside the quoting tool that automatically applies a premium surcharge to high-demand peak booking windows (e.g., Friday mornings) and offers lower base rates for low-demand windows (e.g., Tuesday afternoons). * Staggered Shift Reporting Engine: To eliminate office congestion and loitering, the scheduling engine must monitor traffic data and destination locations to calculate staggered morning reporting windows. The system must automatically notify individual teams via push notifications exactly when to report to the locker facility to collect company assets or route them straight from their homes using personal vehicles. * Dynamic Route Optimization: Deploy a scheduling system with intelligent routing engines capable of mapping the most efficient paths for 6 to 12 field teams every morning. * Dynamic Fleet Assignment Engine: Build a custom fleet routing dashboard within our central data engine (Airtable/SmartSuite). The system must automatically calculate total daily route mileage every morning, rank all active routes (up to 12) from highest to lowest mileage, and assign our company cars to the highest-mileage routes automatically based on a dynamic fleet database table capable of expanding to an unlimited number of vehicles. * Preference & Capability Filters: Embed user-level data tags to track individual employee vehicle constraints, including: Prefers Personal Car, No Car Available, and Non-Driver. * Fair Personal Car Rotation: For routes utilizing personal vehicles, create a rotation counter that logs historical driving shifts per employee to ensure fair distribution of driving responsibilities. * Self-Service Customer Event Portal: Implement an interactive portal allowing clients to modify scheduling parameters based on dynamic personal disruptions (e.g., vacations, upcoming parties, incoming house guests). The system must autonomously adjust route time slots and process real-time pricing recalculations based on these temporary variables. 🛑 Part B: Frequency Discount Enforcement, Subcontractor Routing, & On-Site Change Orders * The Tracking Database: Build a custom database tracking trigger to monitor ongoing client booking behaviors. * The Scammer Rule: We reserve our deepest discounts for high-frequency cleanings (e.g., weekly, based on an expected target rate of $150/hour). If a client signs up for a weekly/biweekly discount but intentionally reschedules to effectively get a monthly cleaning rhythm, they must be caught. * Auto-Price Adjustment: If a client flips their weekly/biweekly schedule into a monthly cadence twice within a rolling 12-month window, the system must trigger an automated alert and permanently adjust their ongoing service price to our standard monthly rate. * Proximity Micro-Jobs: Our baseline job price is $125. Build a geo-radius rule that bypasses this minimum requirement ONLY if an inbound lead requests a small job located within immediate block-by-block proximity to an existing scheduled cleaning route on that specific day. * Bottom-Feeder Filter & New Card Enforcement: The quoting interface must vet client budget expectations early in the pipeline. If a user inputs low-value budget targets (e.g., a whole home clean for $50), the system must immediately trigger a polite, automated pop-up confirming our structural minimums before letting them advance. Cash and check payment paths must be completely restricted for all new customer sign-ups; a valid card method is required to finalize bookings. * Real-Time Undercharging Yield Audit: Build an automated revenue auditing formula that cross-references Stripe invoice payouts against actual mobile app GPS time spent cleaning in the home. If a client's flat recurring frequency rate results in a real-time yield below our target metric of $150 per hour, the account must be auto-flagged for an ongoing rate increase. * On-Site Change Order & Client Authorization Workflows: Construct an internal mobile app form for team captains to submit property condition overestimates or client add-on service requests directly on site. The system must programmatically calculate precise additional time/pricing adjustments, deploy an instant SMS/email text to the client featuring a secure tokenized digital authorization signature link, scale the active Stripe card hold, and automatically update downstream route timelines on autopilot. * Automated Subcontractor Routing Loop & Window Restriction: Configure the frontend quoting engine to lock out internal cleaning teams from large, high-reach exterior window wash tasks, limiting employee window operations strictly to small, standard interior windows and standard household sliding glass doors. If a customer adds premium Carpet Cleaning, Tile Cleaning, or heavy Oversized Window Detailing, the backend data engine must trigger an autonomous subcontractor communication workflow via Make webhooks, pushing job metrics instantly to our whitelisted network of third-party specialty professionals while queuing up margin-retained payment splits. 📊 Part C: Funnel Analytics, B2B Enterprise Portals, & Legacy Migration * Drop-off Milestone Tracking: Configure event webhooks at every stage of the digital quote tool (Contact Entry ➔ API Address Check ➔ Price Quote Display ➔ Date & Credit Card Collection) to audit and evaluate conversion bottlenecks. * B2B Contract Onboarding Framework: Construct a dedicated commercial account portal that supports high-volume scheduling configurations (apartments/low-income turnarounds) and routes corporate or government agency workflows through an automated DocuSign/SignWell verification chain, assigning Net-30 invoicing permissions upon digital execution. Use code with caution. • One-Time Legacy Data Migration: Extract all legacy database customer profile records, billing address items, household preferences, and pet notes out of our current Salesforce system via manual CSV exports. Build a data-mapping script to securely clean and import this historical information into our new Airtable platform prior to launch. 🕒 Part D: Workforce Tracking, Corporate Device MDM, & Live GPS Geofencing • Corporate Device Deployment (Non-BYOD): Onboard 15 field employees into a company-provided smartphone ecosystem. Physical hardware tracking on employee personal devices is strictly prohibited to maintain professional standards. • Mobile Device Management (MDM) Lockdowns: Configure a Mobile Device Management framework (such as Hexnode or Miradore) to place all fleet phones into strict "Kiosk Mode," restricting device interfaces exclusively to work apps and secure internal AI lines. • Masked Voice and Text Communications: Configure the phone framework to allow direct two-way voice calling and text communication between field teams and customers. All voice calls and text threads must be programmatically masked, tracked, and recorded via our programmable telecommunication engine (Twilio Flex), completely hiding real phone numbers on both sides to preserve client account security. • Live Path Geofencing & Time Theft Tracking: Configure background mobile GPS tracking corridors. The database engine must cross-reference real-time traffic maps against field coordinates. If an employee vehicle breaks the route boundary to run personal errands or execute unauthorized travel stops, the system must autonomously log the violation. • Intelligent GPS Filtering: The tracking logic must implement dynamic filters to distinguish legitimate stops from time theft, incorporating a 10-minute/1-mile transit buffer to account for traffic anomalies, an automatic tracking pause when a captain clicks "Start Meal Break", and a geofenced Point-of-Interest (POI) whitelist that categorizes stops at commercial fueling stations as authorized. • Automated Tardiness & Compliance Warnings: Program the engine to compile a daily "GPS Compliance Report" of all violators (including morning locker check-in tardiness and house arrival window misses not caused by traffic). The system must autonomously issue digital written performance warnings via text/email directly to the employee's file, deploying in their preferred language (English or Spanish). • Data Security Firewalls: Configure strict mobile interface rules to hide sensitive customer database details, customer phone numbers, and service pricing from field teams. • One-Click Gusto Sync: Map accurate bi-weekly timesheets and mileage data fields directly to Gusto for single-click payroll processing. 🔏 Part E: Automated Client Communications & Number Masking • Replacement of Legacy Systems: Completely replace our legacy client communication tools (Salesforce Dispatch and Text Request) with our standalone system. • Instant Quote Response: Ensure the quoting tool instantly updates leads with an optimized cleaning date option and an embedded Stripe payment processing credential link within 60 seconds of submission. • Automated Scheduling Triggers: Configure the central system to automatically deploy branded text and email reminders to clients exactly 24 hours prior to their scheduled cleaning, embedding strict late-cancellation notification alerts and fee warnings. • Real-Time Dispatch Notifications: Program automated text alerts to deploy instantly when a field team marks themselves as "On the Way" or "Arrived" inside the mobile workforce application. 📋 Part F: Quality Control, Supply Tracking, & California Labor Compliance • Amazon Inventory Integration: Build a webhook integration connecting our Amazon Business purchasing account to our central Airtable database to automatically log incoming stock volumes and material costs. • Asset Abuse & Loss Tracking: Expand the app's daily checkout/check-in module to strictly audit cloth losses and equipment breakages. The system must generate weekly variance reports identifying exactly which teams have the highest rates of product consumption, lost microfiber cloths, and broken or abused equipment. • Digital Checklists & Sign-Offs: Build customized, house-specific quality checklists within the mobile app. Crews working in pairs (Captain + Teammate) must complete these forms, requiring digital sign-offs from the team captain and client before closing a job. • Mandated Break Reminders: Configure automated push notifications to deploy twice daily reminding field personnel exactly when their statutory rest breaks and meal periods must be taken under CA law. • Shift Attestation & Compliant Archiving: Embed a mandatory compliance questionnaire into the daily clock-out sequence. The team captain must digitally certify that all legally required rest and meal periods were taken, eliminating paper sign-out workflows entirely. Securely archive all signed break certifications and field timesheets for the legally mandated lookback period (3 to 4 years). ✉️ Part G: Email Lead Parsing & Routing Loops • Data Scraping: Build an automated email parsing framework (via Zapier Parser or Mailparser.io) to extract lead details from incoming local website notifications and Google Local Services Ads (LSA) to route back to our database logic. 📢 Part H: Fully Autonomous Regional Marketing & Holiday Automation • Territorial Geofencing Filters: Establish a master "Territory Whitelist" database of approved city names and zip codes within our National City franchise boundaries to keep marketing campaigns tightly contained within our legal borders. • Capacity-Driven Smart Blast: If open appointment blocks are detected on the calendar for the upcoming 48 hours (including openings generated by client vacation skips), the system must autonomously generate a targeted SMS/email promotional offer and push it to unbooked leads, past clients, and historical unbooked opportunities to rapidly fill scheduling gaps at a reduced rate. • Automated Holiday Loops: Program the marketing scheduler to detect seasonal calendar triggers (e.g., Mother's Day, Veterans Day, Christmas). The system must autonomously command OpenAI to write targeted ad copies, link specific custom stripe discount codes, and publish them via ActiveCampaign and social media channels on an automated pre-holiday schedule. • Hands-Free Ad Generation & Social Publishing: Set up a recurring AI workflow that commands OpenAI to automatically generate localized, conversion-oriented ad copies for Facebook, Instagram, and Google LSA targeting the National City regional market, automatically scheduling them to post via Buffer or Hootsuite API. • Hands-Free Newsletter Loop: Establish an autonomous monthly workflow where the AI drafts a full email newsletter. The text must automatically load into Mailchimp or ActiveCampaign, compile into a template, and deploy directly to your client mailing lists. • Dynamic Local Website Updates: Build a scraping loop or RSS filter that monitors community events and organization schedules within our whitelisted cities. Pair this community data with positive weekly client reviews and feed it into OpenAI to autonomously draft and publish localized blog posts or update website sidebars via API (e.g., WordPress API) to keep our local web content fresh and regionally relevant. • Automated Review Streaming: Configure a script that copies raw text from all internal 5-star client feedback surveys and pushes them directly into a dynamic testimonial widget on our local website homepage. 📊 Part I: Performance Analytics & Employee Incentive Scorecard • Automated Feedback Collection: Trigger immediate, automated post-clean text/email surveys to clients upon job completion, routing 1-to-5 star responses directly into team records. • Comprehensive Performance Scorecard*: Build an automated data calculation dashboard in Airtable that scores and ranks teams weekly across five distinct variables to determine performance incentive bonuses: 1. Average customer satisfaction rating. 2. Supply and equipment care efficiency (lowest product usage/lowest breakage rates). 3. Revenue productivity index (Time-in-home vs. travel downtime). 4. Compliance privacy (taking CA rest and meal periods exactly on schedule). 5. Clock discipline (on-time morning clock-ins and rapid end-of-day office clock-outs). 🤖 Part J: Interactive AI Agents & Autonomous Voice Receptionist • AI-Driven Voice Migration: Architect an intelligent voice infrastructure (migrating away from RingCentral to a highly programmable developer environment like Twilio, integrated with Vapi.ai, Retell AI, or Bland.ai) to handle automated inbound and outbound phone operations during receptionist breaks. • Voice Receptionist & Field Relay: The voice agent must be capable of providing verbal price quotes to inbound client leads via real-time public records lookups, taking inbound client updates, automatically mapping the caller to the active field team, translating verbal requests into Spanish if required, and instantly pushing text alerts to the field team's mobile interface. • Live Quote Page AI Assistant: Embed a specialized, customer-facing conversational AI agent directly onto our website quoting tool page. The agent must handle routine customer FAQs in real time during the quote process, using a human escalation protocol to instantly dispatch an urgent SMS notification to the office team for high-priority or unresolvable scenarios. • Internal Staff Information AI: Build a secure internal information bot for employees. This bot must process natural language text queries, allowing workers to quickly text and ask about gate keys and door lockbox codes while out in the field. 🎁 Part K: Secure Payments, Card Surcharging, Gifting Ecosystem, & Salesforce RPA Bots • Pre-Service Authorization Logic: Configure the Stripe payment engine to automatically execute temporary credit card authorization holds the afternoon prior to a scheduled service. The system must verify card activity and reserve the required funds without processing a live premature charge. • Automated Credit Card Processing Fee Surcharging: Configure Stripe to automatically calculate standard merchant card processing fees and append them directly to consumer invoices as an itemized processing surcharge line item at layout completion. • Automated Payment Failure Workflows: If a pre-service card authorization fails due to expiration, insufficient funds, or standard card declines, the system must trigger an autonomous, multi-channel response: deploy an immediate SMS and email alert to the client with an embedded, tokenized card-update link, place a temporary operational hold on the field team's schedule, and trigger an urgent office notification if the client fails to securely update details within a pre-set 2-hour grace window. • Dynamic Gift Card Ecosystem: Construct a checkout module on our website letting users securely buy custom digital gift cards for external recipients via Stripe. The tool must automatically generate unique, tracked redemptions within our system and handle automated recipient email deployments. • Hands-Free Human, Pet Birthday, & Anniversary Tracker: Configure the data engine to support individual client profiles as well as a relational pet sub-table (Pet Name, Pet Type, Pet Birthday). The system must autonomously check for active human birthdays, customer service anniversaries, and registered pets' birthdays every morning to generate a 10% discount code in Stripe for birthdays or a 15% discount for service anniversaries, email the digital certificate to the user, and automatically deduct the value from their upcoming recurring invoice. All customer-facing holiday and birthday communication pipelines must dynamically support bilingual delivery options based on client language tags. • Automated Salesforce Work Order Data Injection: Because Salesforce API inbound access is fully locked, construct a secure browser-level Robotic Process Automation (RPA) macro bot (via Axiom.ai, Bardeen, or Make.com browser environments). Every evening, the bot must programmatically extract successful booking metrics, variable duration calendar blocks, and precise revenue/productivity variables from our standalone database, securely open a browser window to log into our franchise Salesforce instance, and fill out the extensive work order accounting ledger fields and calendar slots flawlessly without human entry. 🎓 Part L: Automated Learning Management System (LMS) & Compliance Tracking • LMS Platform Integration: Integrate an API-driven training platform (such as TalentLMS or Trainual) into our central Airtable database engine. • Dynamic Performance Learning Paths & Refresher Triggers: Build automated triggers that read internal data and client reviews. If a team receives low feedback on a specific cleaning task, high supply waste, excessive equipment breakage, or omits standard check-out procedures, the system must autonomously enroll those specific employees into tailored refresher training modules and temporarily lock field-app clock-in permissions until completed. • Comprehensive Spanish Language Localization: Ensure all internal employee training paths, compliance modules, automated performance warnings, text/email notifications, and internal AI info bots feature a complete Spanish-language option, dynamically deploying in the employee's preferred language based on user profile tags. • California Statutory Compliance Tracking: Standardize mandatory onboarding tracks for California law requirements, including Workplace Violence Prevention and Sexual Harassment Prevention, with automated recurring enrollment features set to exact statutory frequencies. 🌟 Part M: Reputation Management Funneling & Weekly Operational AI Reports • Conditional Review Gate Routing: Implement an automated post-clean survey loop that filters reviews. Submissions rating 4–5 stars must immediately route to our Google LSA and Google Business review profiles via automated SMS links. Submissions rating 1–3 stars must bypass public channels, capture raw feedback privately, alert management, and programmatically apply a "Future Service Credit" inside Stripe against their next scheduled invoice total. • Weekly Executive AI Reports: Build a recurring weekend workflow utilizing the OpenAI API to ingest all weekly client surveys, downtime records, cloth variance logs, and equipment damage counts. The agent must pipeline this data and email a comprehensive operational summary highlighting weekly successes, operational failures, and concrete procedural recommendations to improve franchise quality. 📦 Part N: Standalone Smart Locker Hub & Automated Access Distribution • Dynamic Access Key Generation: Connect our smart locker hardware API (such as SentriLock or Master Lock API) to our central Airtable database. The system must automatically generate unique, time-sensitive access pin codes for individual team lockers every morning. • Automated Dispatch Triggers: The system must text the locker codes directly to team captains alongside their dynamic vehicle assignments, ensuring teams can independently withdraw their specialized equipment bins, keys, and fresh cloths without office oversight. • EV Fleet Charging Telematics Integration: Integrate a vehicle telematics checker (via Smartcar or native fleet APIs) into our nightly logic. The system must automatically verify that all company EVs are actively docked and charging at the storage facility grid by 10:00 PM, dynamically launching an urgent restock push alert to the logistics runner if an operational vehicle is detected as unplugged. • Automated Return Verification Logs: Configure the evening clock-out dashboard inside the workforce management app to enforce an immutable checkout protocol. Before the time clock stops, the app must mandate a microfiber rag reconciliation count log, an equipment breakage report form, and a smartphone photo verification upload of the organized locker interior, paired with background GPS geofence locks confirming physical presence at the locker facility. • Logistics Runner Dashboard: Build a remote logistics interface in Airtable that aggregates evening team checkout logs. The system must display precise rag and chemical replenishment values per locker for the midnight logistics runner, auto-clearing alerts once they hit "Restock Complete" to generate the next morning's smart pin codes. 🏆 Part O: Automated Employee Rewards, Milestone Gifting, & Gusto Wage Scaling • Instant Performance Gift Card Integration: Connect our central Airtable scorecard logic to an automated digital corporate gifting API (such as Tango Card or Blackhawk Network). On Day 91, the database must change worker profiles to full status, notify the employee via text, and programmatically scale their base hourly wage rate within Gusto based on pre-configured corporate parameters. • Global Wage Rules & Minimum Wage Scaling Engine: Build a centralized fleet-wide wage adjustments dashboard. When structural California state, local San Diego minimum wage guidelines, or local market competitiveness rates shift, modifying the master baseline table must instantly update all corresponding employee profiles and scale active hourly pay rates within the Gusto payroll infrastructure automatically without manual account data entry. 👥 Part P: Standalone Recruitment Portal & Behavioral Screening Funnel • Custom Candidate Intake & Situational Assessment Portal: Build an embedded frontend application form on our local website to capture applicant backgrounds, documentation, and specific situational judgment paragraph responses regarding on-site crew conflicts and customer escalations. • AI Toxicity & Risk-Filtering Engine: Integrate an OpenAI API workflow that instantly ingests raw text answers from applicants to audit for underlying behavioral markers, passive-aggression, blame-shifting, or toxic team traits. The engine must dynamically calculate and log a Behavioral Risk Score directly onto the candidate card in Airtable to filter out troublesome profiles. • Time-Locked Commitment Gateways: Program automated text/email pathways that challenge high-scoring applicants to complete a secondary video-response screening hurdle within a strict 24-hour completion window, programmatically archiving unmotivated candidates who miss deadlines to guarantee high training-retention rates. • Integrated Background & DMV Verification API: Connect the recruitment dashboard via API to an automated background screening infrastructure (such as Checkr or Turn) to programmatically audit records for indicators of theft, workplace violence, or severe motor vehicle violations, instantly applying a hard disqualification block on high-risk files. • Variable Split-Shift & Multi-Task Payroll Architectures: Configure the mobile workforce tracking application (Connecteam/Timeero) to support variable everyday scheduling and a compliant California labor multi-task clock layout. Senior AI Engineer Needed – Build a Reusable AI Knowledge Platform with ChatGPT I’m looking for an experienced AI engineer to build a reusable AI knowledge platform that uses ChatGPT as the primary interface. This is not a chatbot project. My goal is to create a long-term knowledge system that allows me to have natural, ongoing conversations with ChatGPT about large collections of research material. The platform should be reusable across many different projects by simply connecting a new Google Drive folder. The Vision I produce long-form nonfiction content including podcasts, documentaries, television, and investigative projects. Every project accumulates a large collection of source material. Examples include: * Interview transcripts * News articles * PDFs * Books * Research documents * Production notes * Images * OCR documents * Metadata * Additional research added over time I want each project to become its own AI knowledge base that I can converse with naturally through ChatGPT. Google Drive is the Source of Truth Google Drive should serve as the primary repository for all project materials. Each project should correspond to one or more Google Drive folders. The platform should: * Monitor Google Drive folders for new or updated files. * Automatically ingest and index new content. * Preserve source references back to the original files. * Keep the AI knowledge base synchronized as files are added or updated. * Allow me to create a new AI project simply by connecting a new Google Drive folder. I do not want to manually upload documents into another application. Google Drive should remain my normal workflow. ChatGPT Integration (Primary Requirement) The primary user interface will be ChatGPT. I do not want a custom chat application. Instead, I want ChatGPT to function as an intelligent research partner that has access to my project knowledge bases. The experience should feel like this: * I create or select a project. * I open ChatGPT. * I ask questions in natural language. * ChatGPT retrieves information from that project’s knowledge base as needed. * It remembers the current conversation and builds upon previous questions naturally. * It cites sources whenever possible. I should be able to access the same projects from multiple computers while logged into my ChatGPT account. Core Requirements The platform should: * Support unlimited independent projects. * Support many different document and media types. * Automatically ingest new material over time. * Return accurate, source-backed answers. * Be modular and reusable. * Be well documented. * Be designed for long-term expansion. Future Automation Phase 1 is focused on building the knowledge platform and ChatGPT integration. However, the architecture should be designed so it can later integrate with automation tools such as Make.com. For example: * A new transcript is added to Google Drive. * The knowledge base updates automatically. * AI extracts factoids, quotes, clips, summaries, or social media content. * Those outputs can be passed into additional automated workflows. I do not need these automations built in Phase 1, but I want the platform designed with this future in mind. Existing Progress I already have a working proof of concept built with Python, FastAPI, OpenAI APIs, and OpenAI Vector Stores. It currently indexes documents and performs semantic search. I’m open to improving or replacing parts of the implementation if there is a better long-term architecture. Ideal Experience I’m looking for someone with experience in: * Python * OpenAI APIs * Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) * AI knowledge systems * Vector databases * FastAPI (or similar) * AI agent architecture * Google Drive API * Production software architecture When Applying Please include: * Examples of similar AI knowledge or RAG systems you’ve built. * GitHub or portfolio. * How you would architect this platform. * Whether you would continue using OpenAI Vector Stores or recommend another architecture (and why). * Your estimated timeline and budget. I’m looking for someone interested in building a reusable AI knowledge platform that will become a core part of my company’s research and content development workflow.
