Proposals: 20 to 50
AI Automation Engineer — Build a Unified YouTube & Content Pipeline for an Established Podcast
Hourly - Expert - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, not_sure
What this is I run Beyond The Ordinary, a spirituality and consciousness interview show I've been doing for 13 years. Three live interviews a week, a large direct email list, a paid membership, and an archive of over 1,200 recorded conversations that currently do nothing for me after the broadcast ends. I want to fix that. Not with a tool. With a system I own, and eventually license to other hosts. This engagement has two jobs that share one foundation: Job 1 : YouTube growth. Each Hero interview becomes a long-term discoverable asset. Optimized titles, thumbnail concepts, descriptions, tags, mid-form clip packages for YouTube. Built from real keyword data, not model guesses. Job 2: Content distribution. Each Hero transcript becomes a full asset stack: long-form SEO article, email draft, social carousels with finished images, LinkedIn version, metadata. All in my voice. All sourced only from what I actually said. One foundation. Two branches. You're building the whole thing. I'm also building this to scale. The system needs to support multiple hosts down the road, each with their own voice profile, brand inputs, and SEO targets. The infrastructure stays shared. The voice and brand layer swaps per host. Architect for that from the start. Before any building starts — Phase 0 Day 1-2, nothing gets built until this is done. Audit my YouTube channel: titles, descriptions, tags, thumbnails, end screens, playlists, channel sections, About page. Identify the top 20 quick wins on existing videos that could be retitled or re-thumbnailed right now. Audit beyondtheordinaryshow.com: schema markup, internal linking, topic cluster structure, page speed, indexability. Deliver a written document with prioritized recommendations. I want to know what's already working before we automate around it. The system, stage by stage Foundation (Weeks 1-2) Transcript intake agent triggers on every new YouTube upload. It pulls the transcript, strips filler, removes member names, flags confidential sections for my review, identifies topic shifts with timestamps, and scores emotionally intense moments worth pulling as clips or quotes. Clean structured record saves to the database automatically. All three weekly interviews are ingested. But nothing beyond ingestion happens automatically. I flag one interview per week as Hero in the database, and that flag is what triggers everything downstream. Database: one record per interview, status pipeline from raw through published, linked tables for clips, quotes, SEO articles, keyword research, and social assets. Recommend Notion or Airtable with reasoning: I'm open to either, or something better if you can make the case. Hero Packaging (Weeks 3-4) When I drop the Hero flag, the packaging agent fires. Before generating anything, it pulls real data: DataForSEO for search volume, People Also Ask, related searches, and competition. YouTube Data API for what's actually performing in my niche. Outputs: 10 title variants, 3 thumbnail concept briefs with emotional hook described, optimized YouTube description with timestamps and keywords, tag clusters, recommended cold-open timestamp. On YouTube optimization scoring: VidIQ has no public API so manual use stays manual. I'm open to integrating 1of10 or TubeBuddy if they offer meaningful automation at this stage. Include your recommendation on what's actually automatable here and what the tradeoffs are. Don't assume I'm locked into any specific tool. Clip Extraction (Weeks 3-4, parallel) The agent reads the transcript's flagged moments and outputs a prioritized clip package for a video editor. Clip format is 5-15 minute mid-form segments, not 60-second Shorts. These are substantial standalone conversations, not highlight reels. Each clip gets a suggested title, hook line, timestamps, and suggested cuts. Hand-off format should be immediately usable by an editor without interpretation. YouTube is the primary distribution target. Keep the architecture clean enough to add other platforms, but don't build for them now, unless it's an easy integration for short-form clips and carousels for IG and LinkedIn. Content Distribution (Weeks 3-4, parallel) Same Hero transcript, separate branch. Agent surfaces the three strongest article angles, quotes worth pulling, recurring themes, and questions the transcript answers. I pick the angle. The agent never picks for me. Once I've chosen, it writes 1,500 to 2,500 words in my voice using only what's in the transcript. I write the opening, the truth at the center, and the invitation at the end. The agent handles the body. Also produces: email draft, carousel scripts, LinkedIn version, YouTube description, SEO metadata (title under 60 characters, meta description around 155, slug, internal link suggestions to related archive interviews, FAQ block). Finished Social Images (Weeks 5-6) The carousel produces finished images, not just scripts. I'll have a designer build the templates: carousel opener, body, closer, quote card, and clip cover. Your job is wiring the agent to fill those templates via API, render the images, save them to the database, and surface them for my review. Text approval happens before image rendering. I'm not burning through renders on copy I'm going to rewrite anyway. Individual slide regeneration needs to work without rerunning the whole carousel. Build v1 on Bannerbear. The architecture needs a clean migration path to Canva Connect API when I bring other hosts onto the platform, brand inputs swap per host, infrastructure stays shared. SEO Article Engine (Month 2) Agent takes Hero transcript and topic, queries DataForSEO for real search queries, People Also Ask, related searches, and long-tail variants in my niche, then produces a long-form article structured around actual search questions, not the interview's chronological flow. H2 headers built from real Google queries. Embedded video placement. Internal link suggestions to related archive interviews. Future: Analytics Feedback Loop (Month 4-6, architect for it now) Don't build it yet. Agent 5 will analyze CTR, retention, search traffic, and conversion patterns and feed that back into the packaging and article agents. Architect so this plugs in without rebuilding anything foundational. The line that cannot move The agent does not invent. Not stories, not quotes, not statistics, not spiritual claims, not anything I didn't actually say on the recording. Build this rule into the prompts at every stage. Test against it deliberately. The moment the archive contains fabricated wisdom attributed to me, the whole project is a liability. This is not a preference. It's the constraint everything else runs on. The agent also doesn't publish to my site or send emails to my list without me. Human gate stays in place at every output. Stack I have preferences, but I'm not locked in. If you can make a strong case for a different tool at any layer, orchestration, database, transcription, or image rendering, please make it. What matters is that the system works, scales to multiple hosts cleanly, and that I own it. Current working preferences: Orchestration: n8n or Make.com — recommend one with reasoning Language agents: Claude API for writing-heavy work, OpenAI for utility tasks Transcription: Whisper or Descript API — explain the cost tradeoff at my volume (3 interviews/week) Database: Notion or Airtable — recommend one with reasoning SEO data: DataForSEO YouTube: YouTube Data API for upload triggers, metadata, and competitor research YouTube optimization: Open to 1of10, TubeBuddy, or your recommendation — include what's actually automatable Social images: Bannerbear v1, Canva Connect API migration path Publishing: WordPress API (optional final stage — I still hit publish) Include estimated DataForSEO monthly cost at my volume in your proposal. Voice tuning I'll give you 10-15 of my strongest pieces. Study them until you can articulate what makes my writing mine, then encode that into the prompts at every stage — not just the article agent. Every output this system produces needs to sound like me. This is the hardest part of the engagement. It's also what determines whether the system is actually usable. If you treat it as a prompt you can write in an afternoon, the drafts will read like AI and the engagement will stall. The system also needs to support other hosts eventually. Each host gets their own voice profile, trained on their own writing samples, producing output that sounds like them. Build the voice layer so it's parameterized from the start, not baked into a single hardcoded prompt. Deliverables Phase 0 written audit (YouTube channel + website) Working orchestration scenarios for each agent across all four phases Database workspace with full structure, status pipeline, and views System prompts for each agent, version-controlled in a doc I own Loom walkthrough of the full system (30-45 minutes) Written runbook: how to use it, how to modify prompts, how to debug when something breaks Two weeks post-launch support for fixes and tuning What I'll provide API keys (Claude, OpenAI, YouTube, database, DataForSEO, Bannerbear) Style guide document with voice rules and banned patterns 10-15 writing samples in my actual voice 5 example interviews already published for testing on real content Access to YouTube channel data and website Who I want You've shipped production AI workflows for content businesses. Not experiments. Real systems handling real content at volume, with error handling, branching logic, rate limiting, and retries built in. You can explain technical decisions in plain English. You understand the tradeoffs between tools, not just how to use them. You understand prompt engineering well enough to tune voice across multiple hosts, not just write generic prompts and call it done. You think about architecture. This system is going to grow. The choices you make in weeks 1-2 will either make that easy or painful. Required: at least two production multi-step AI agent workflows (be ready to show them), advanced n8n or Make.com, direct API work with Claude or OpenAI, YouTube Data API, Notion or Airtable API. Bonus: Bannerbear, Descript, WordPress API, DataForSEO, 1of10 or TubeBuddy API experience, podcast or creator-economy workflow background. Budget and timeline Fixed price. Phased payments tied to Phase 0 audit plus four build phases. Realistic timeline: 6-8 weeks. Submit a phase-by-phase breakdown so I can see what each milestone costs. Include your tool recommendations and reasoning. I want to see how you think, not just what you'd charge. I'd rather pay more for someone who has done this than less for someone learning on my project. Lowball bids will be filtered out.

Hourly: $40-$90 - Expert - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
We are developing a suite of AI agents for 3D modeling, interior design, architecture, and procurement. These agents power customer-facing experiences while also interacting with our internal systems and automating the workflows behind that experience. The work is not a generic chatbot project. We need agents that can reason over product data, call tools, coordinate across services, retain shared memory, and produce reliable outputs that can be used in production workflows. This is an existing product and codebase. You will work with our team to design, implement, test, and improve agents that are useful, observable, and safe to deploy. Scope of Work You will help build and improve agents that interact with our product, design, and project systems. The initial areas include: - SKU ingestion agent: gathers and validates data for new products in our system, enriches SKU attributes, identifies missing information, and prepares product records for downstream use. - Design agent: selects products based on a user's input, room context, design preferences, constraints, and available catalog data. - Project agent: keeps users informed via text or voice, tracks scope and budget, coordinates project status, and supports procurement workflows. The work will include: - Designing workflows, tools, prompts, state, and evals. - Implementing APIs, databases, and customer-facing surfaces. - Building reliable tool-calling, retries, and handoffs. - Improving product-data quality, normalization, and retrieval. - Shipping production code, tests, logging, and docs. - Measuring behavior with evals, traces, and regressions. Technical Expectations We expect this work to involve using TypeScript, Python, and Mastra to create backend services, customer-facing applications, and data systems. You should be comfortable working across application code, agent orchestration, APIs, and databases. Qualifications - Strong full-stack engineering background with production systems experience. - Experience building AI agents, tool-using LLMs, or workflow automation systems. - Strong TypeScript experience, ideally including agent frameworks such as Mastra. - Python experience for data processing, service integration, or model-adjacent workflows. - Experience integrating LLMs with real databases, APIs, queues, customer-facing workflows, or internal tools. - Comfort with structured product data, catalogs, SKUs, variants, and data quality. - Ability to design evals, trace behavior, debug failures, and improve reliability over time. - Good judgment around human-in-the-loop workflows, guardrails, and permissions. - Clear written communication and ability to explain tradeoffs and decisions. - AWS, PostgreSQL, and voice/SMS integration experience are nice to have.

Proposals: 10 to 15
Automate Chess with WinBoard/UCI
Fixed-price - Expert - Est. Budget: $750
Project: automate chess processes using WinBoard/UCI and orchestrate local AI agents for massive custom variant tournaments. Main skill: Master Windows PowerShell, LM Studio, use of Winboard and Fairy Stockfish. The project involves creating a system that can efficiently manage and execute large-scale chess tournaments with custom rules: Core Objective Build a local orchestration system that can: Generate and run massive batches of custom tournaments. Use custom armies (often asymmetrical) with variable piece sets. Start games from a FEN database generated from all defined piece combinations. Export all games to PGN files, with filenames linked to the corresponding FEN database. Evaluate and validate a handicap formula based on piece-count asymmetry and relative piece values (in centipawns). Technical Scope The system should automate: Tournament creation and launch (WinBoard/UCI workflow). Generate Fen data for each armies combinations. Save Fen Data using Letters and their corresponding Betza Notation: For example White plays with extra Q and Black plays with Hawk and Unicorn. The generated Data will be saved on file named Q vs HH then add Betza Notation for each extra piece, Q = Q etc Engine setup and execution (Fairy-Stockfish compatible). FEN database ingestion as tournament starting positions. Match scheduling across many custom army configurations. PGN output management with consistent naming conventions. PGN should have the same name than the FEN Data. If FEN Data is big (for example superior than 500 positions, the data will be split to multiples of 500, then the data will be remaned incrementally 1 etc. Local execution on Windows (PowerShell-friendly automation). Tournament Design Logic Each side can be assigned custom piece pools (example ranges: 1 to 20 custom pieces available to chose from per side). The armies can be 1 to 4 pieces, For each generated army: Every custom piece has a predefined relative value in centipawns (cp). Armies are frequently asymmetrical (example: White has 1 very strong piece vs Black has 3 weaker pieces). In many scenarios, White’s single piece may have a higher raw value than Black’s total piece value. Black may receive a handicap adjustment because it plays with more pieces. Handicap Model to Validate (Main Experimental Goal) The primary experiment is to validate whether the handicap correction below is appropriate: 1 extra piece: +100 cp handicap 2 extra pieces: +250 cp handicap (100 + 150) 3 extra pieces: +350 cp handicap (250 + 100) 4 or more extra pieces: capped at +350 cp This handicap is applied to the side with more pieces (typically Black in asymmetrical setups), and the project’s main scientific objective is to test if this model produces balanced and meaningful outcomes across large tournament samples. Expected Outcome A robust local pipeline that can repeatedly: design custom tournaments, run them at scale, store clean PGN datasets, and generate evidence to confirm or reject the handicap formula.

Proposals: 50+
Production RAG System for Document Intelligence (LLM, Vector Search, API Integrations, WhatsApp Bot)
Fixed-price - Expert - Est. Budget: $10K
Description I'm building a production-grade Contract Intelligence Engine for a luxury travel advisory firm and am looking for a senior engineer to own the technical build end-to-end (or anchor a small build alongside me). What it does: An advisor asks a question in plain language — e.g., "couple going to Tokyo mid-December, somewhere nice with breakfast" — and gets back the relevant hotel contracts, applicable rates, computed margins, conditions (blackout dates, eligibility, cancellation terms), and citations back to the source PDF. The corpus is ~500–1,000 contracts today, scaling to thousands. Architecture (retrieval-based, four layers): Ingestion — parse contract PDFs to text (clean machine-readable PDFs, with an OCR fallback), generate embeddings, and extract structured fields (hotel, location, currency, pricing model, validity dates) via a frontier LLM. Store text + vector + structured fields together. Retrieval — embed the query, surface the most relevant contracts via hybrid semantic similarity + structured filters, so only the matching subset is sent to the model and cost stays flat as the corpus grows. Public-rate comparison + transfers — integrate TravelWits / Sabre (public rate lookup for margin comparison) and Rolzo (car-transfer quotes) as sidecars using the client's API credentials. Answer generation — frontier model (Claude as primary) reads the actual contract text, flags restrictions, and cites the governing clause for every material fact. Prompt caching for follow-ups. Critical architectural boundary: all financial figures (net/gross rates, margins, taxes, fees) are computed by a deterministic backend pricing engine, never by the LLM. The model handles intent extraction and presentation only. The financial layer is held to a 0% error tolerance against a test suite; general Q&A to a 95% pass threshold. Other key components: A property-to-GDS mapping table anchoring contracted hotels to canonical IDs (Sabre Property IDs / GIATA codes) with a fallback "requires manual verification" path — entity resolution, not fuzzy text matching. Delivery through a WhatsApp Business API bot (includes Meta verification), with per-advisor phone-number permissioning and full conversation logging. Backend must be interface-agnostic (web/Slack added later without a rebuild). Standard security posture: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, role-based access, hosted on AWS or equivalent. What I need from you: clean, documented, version-controlled code; architecture docs and an operational runbook maintained as you build (not bolted on at the end); and a system I can fully own and hand off. I handle client strategy, scope, and acceptance criteria — you own the engineering. Must-have experience: Shipped at least one production RAG / document-Q&A system (not just demos) Strong Python, LLM APIs (Anthropic and/or OpenAI), embeddings, and a vector store (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, FAISS, or similar) Third-party REST API integration and data/entity resolution across systems Comfortable separating probabilistic AI from deterministic backend logic Nice to have: WhatsApp Business API / Meta verification experience PDF parsing + OCR pipelines at scale Travel/GDS domain exposure (Sabre, etc.) Prompt caching / cost-optimization for LLM apps Please include links to relevant work (GitHub, deployed projects). Engagement runs ~3 months across defined milestones; I work milestone-based with written acceptance criteria agreed up front.

Fixed-price - Intermediate - Est. Budget: $200
Create an AI-Assisted Static Data Room Organizer and Retrieval Package for Real Estate Development Documents. I need a contractor to build a practical tool that can take one or more master “Inbox” folders of mixed project documents and produce a clean, static, data-room-style folder system and retrieval package for a real estate development / infrastructure project. This is not just automated file sorting. The final deliverable must be a static access and retrieval system that includes organized folders, copied files, a searchable local index, a master manifest, confidence scoring, classification explanations, duplicate detection, and a review queue. The system should allow me to drop all files into a master folder, run the tool, and receive a complete organized data-room package. Required outcome: * Organized folder and subfolder hierarchy. * Original files preserved and not modified. * Files copied into assigned output folders. * CSV/Excel master manifest. * Searchable local HTML index that opens without a server. * Confidence score for each classification. * Short explanation of why each file was classified that way. * Exact and near-duplicate report. * “Needs Review” queue for uncertain files. * Audit/error log showing how each file was processed. * Editable taxonomy so the folder structure can be revised and rerun. An LLM or equivalent semantic reasoning layer is required. This cannot be only a keyword sorter. The system must synthesize file names, extracted text, headings, dates, parties, document type, and context to determine the correct primary folder. The LLM should return assigned folder, confidence score, short rationale, key supporting terms/entities, and review-needed flag. Core workflow: 1. Accept one or more input folders with files/subfolders. 2. Extract text and metadata from each file. 3. OCR scanned PDFs and image-based documents where possible. 4. Classify documents into the defined data-room taxonomy using AI/LLM reasoning. 5. Copy each file into the correct output folder without altering originals. 6. Optionally rename copied files using a clean naming convention. 7. Generate the manifest, searchable HTML index, duplicate report, review queue, and audit log. 8. Allow manual corrections and rerun. Required file types: PDF including scanned PDFs, Word .doc/.docx, Excel .xls/.xlsx, PowerPoint .ppt/.pptx, images .jpg/.png/.tif, text files, and .msg/.eml emails if feasible. Initial folder hierarchy should be editable but include: 00_Admin_Index 01_Project_Overview 02_Land_Control_PSA 03_Title_Survey_ALTA 04_Zoning_Land_Use_Local_Approvals 05_Environmental_RCRA_BRAC_FOSET 06_Wetlands_Streams_USACE 07_Floodplain_Drainage_Stormwater 08_Geotechnical_Soils 09_Civil_Engineering_Site_Planning 10_Power_Utility_AEP_SWEPCO 11_BTM_Generation_BESS_Energy 12_Natural_Gas 13_Water_Wastewater 14_Fiber_Telecom 15_Permitting_FAST41_Federal_State 16_Vendors_Proposals_Budgets 17_Capital_Markets_Investor_Materials 18_Correspondence 19_Unclassified_Review_Queue Each main folder may include subfolders such as: * Agreements / Correspondence / Reports / Maps_Exhibits / Proposals / Cost_Estimates / Permits / Review_Needed, as applicable. The taxonomy should be stored in an editable JSON, YAML, CSV, or Excel config file. Preferred architecture: Local-first or hybrid. Use local extraction, OCR, duplicate detection, and embeddings where possible. Use AI/API classification only where needed or approved. If API use is required, disclose exactly what text/metadata is sent externally and allow local-only or limited-text classification. Possible technologies: Python, Tesseract OCR, PyMuPDF/pdfplumber, python-docx, openpyxl, python-pptx, sentence-transformers, FAISS/Chroma, OpenAI API or other LLM classifier, Streamlit, Flask, or simple desktop GUI. Minimum UI: Command-line tool with clear setup instructions and config file. Preferred UI: Simple local browser or desktop app where I can select input folder, select output folder, choose/edit taxonomy, run classification, view progress, open the review queue, export manifest, generate the static HTML index, and rerun after corrections. Security requirements: * Do not modify originals. * Do not upload documents to third-party cloud services unless explicitly enabled. * Do not retain client documents. * Do not store API keys in plain text. * Use dummy/sample files for testing unless real files are specifically approved. Deliverables: Working tool/app/script, source code, editable taxonomy file, organized folder output generator, CSV/Excel manifest, searchable local HTML index, duplicate report, Needs Review report, error/audit log, installation instructions, user guide, and demo using sample files. Acceptance criteria: The tool successfully processes a mixed master folder, classifies files using LLM/semantic reasoning, copies them into the folder hierarchy, preserves originals, creates a searchable static index, produces an audit-friendly manifest, detects duplicates, and flags uncertain files for review. This is not intended to be a full enterprise data room SaaS platform. I need a practical, reliable static document organization and retrieval tool that prepares a usable real estate development data-room package from a large batch of documents.

Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central On-Prem (v25.5) – Service Contract Invoice Generation Issue
Fixed-price - Intermediate - Est. Budget: $200
Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central On-Prem (v25.5) – Service Contract Invoice Generation Issue ## Project Overview We are running Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central On-Premises with custom extensions developed in-house (BITSL extension). We have a Service Contract that should generate a Service Invoice, but Business Central displays: "There is no invoice to create for this contract" We need an experienced Business Central developer who can analyze the AL customization, identify the root cause, and implement a permanent fix. --- ## Environment * Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central On-Premises * Version: 25.x * SQL Server backend * Custom extension: BITSL * Full SQL access available * Full AL source code available * RDP access can be provided * Backup of database already taken --- ## Affected Contract Contract Number: CON-2627-0000089 Customer: Reserve Bank of India (Customer No. C63191) Contract Description: RBI MPD OVERTIME CHARGES Feb & April 26 --- ## Current Issue When clicking: Create Service Invoice the system throws: "There is no invoice to create for this contract" even though the contract appears invoice-ready. --- ## Investigation Already Performed ### Service Contract Header Verified: * Status = Signed * Change Status = Locked/Released * Contract is not blocked * Billing Allowed = False * DUE invoices = 1 * Next Invoice Date = 01-Jun-2026 * Last Invoice Date = Blank * Invoice Period = 50000 ### Contract Invoice Period List Verified pending invoice period exists: * Start Date = 01-May-2026 * End Date = 31-May-2026 * Amount = 10,820.97 * Invoice Created = No * Service Invoice No = Blank * Destination Document No = Blank ### Service Contract Line Verified: * Line Amount = 10,820.97 * Invoiced To Date = Blank (1753 date) * Contract Expiration Date = 31-May-2026 * Credit Memo Date populated ### SQL Verification Verified directly in SQL: * DUE invoices = 1 * Next Invoice Date populated * Contract Invoice Period record exists * Contract line exists * No invoice has been generated for this period --- ## AL Code Findings Custom action: CreateServiceInvoiceBITSL contains: IF (Rec."DUE invoices" = 0) THEN ERROR('There is no invoice to create for this contract'); Field definition: field(50013; "DUE invoices"; Integer) This is a normal stored field (not a FlowField). Another field: field(50025; "Due Invoices For Report"; Integer) Uses a CalcFormula to count pending records in Contract Invoice Period List where Invoice Created = False. The contract invoice period list clearly contains a pending invoice. --- ## Additional Observations * Updating DUE invoices directly through SQL did not resolve the issue. * Updating Next Invoice Date directly through SQL did not resolve the issue. * BC page shows DUE invoices = 1. * SQL shows DUE invoices = 1. * Yet invoice creation still throws the error. This suggests one of the following: 1. Custom code is recalculating DUE invoices before invoice creation. 2. ServContractManagement_BITSL.CreateInvoice() contains additional validation. 3. Running extension differs from available source code. 4. Contract state logic is inconsistent due to customization. --- ## Required Deliverables 1. Identify root cause. 2. Fix invoice generation for the affected contract. 3. Explain why invoice creation is failing. 4. Validate solution on test environment. 5. Provide deployment steps. 6. Ensure future contracts do not face the same issue. --- ## Required Skills * Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central * AL Development * Business Central On-Prem * SQL Server * Service Management Module * Service Contracts * Service Invoicing * Debugging Custom Extensions

Proposals: 20 to 50
Amazon Bedrock
Hourly: $20 - Intermediate - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Upwork Job Post: Build “Project Aegis” — Amazon Bedrock AI System for Estate Planning Document Review Job Title AWS Bedrock AI Engineer Needed to Build Legal Drafting Review System for Estate Planning Packets Project Name Project Aegis Overview We are a Florida law firm looking to build an internal AI quality-control system named Project Aegis. The goal of Aegis is to help our firm review estate planning packets before they are finalized or sent to clients. These packets usually include documents such as: * Revocable trusts * Wills * Durable powers of attorney * Health care surrogate designations * HIPAA authorizations * Living wills * Related estate planning documents * Engagement letters The packets are usually 120 to 200 pages long and may be in English only or English and Spanish. Aegis should review the drafted packet against the client’s actual information and communications to catch drafting mistakes, inconsistencies, wrong names, repeated names, cross-client contamination, and mismatches between the source information and the final documents. This is not a generic chatbot. This is a secure legal-document review system that must compare drafted documents against source materials and produce a clear issue report for attorney/staff review. Main Goal Build an internal AI system using Amazon Bedrock that can review estate planning packets and flag possible drafting errors before the documents are finalized. The system should compare the estate planning packet against available source materials, including: * Intake forms * Meeting transcripts * Cloud meeting videos * Zoom phone call recordings/transcripts * Outlook emails * Microsoft Teams meetings * Microsoft Teams chat messages * Prior client communications * Related engagement letters * Firm-approved templates/forms The final output should be a structured quality-control report showing possible errors, the document/page/section where the issue appears, the source that supports the correction, and the suggested fix. Examples of Errors Aegis Should Catch Aegis should identify errors such as: 1. Wrong Person in Wrong Role Example: The wife’s name is placed where the child’s name should appear. The system should detect that the role does not match the client intake, meeting transcript, or other source materials. 2. Inappropriate Repetition of Names Example: The wife is listed correctly as spouse, but her name is also mistakenly repeated as a child, trustee, beneficiary, or other role where she does not belong. 3. Cross-Matter Contamination Example: A name from another client matter appears in the current client’s estate planning packet. This is a major issue. The system should flag names, addresses, relatives, fiduciaries, or other facts that do not belong to the current matter. 4. Incorrect Family Relationships Example: A person identified as the client’s child in the draft is actually the client’s spouse, sibling, parent, or unrelated person according to the intake or meeting. 5. Engagement Letter Mismatches The same types of errors can occur in the engagement letter. Aegis should review the engagement letter for: * Wrong client name * Wrong spouse name * Wrong scope of work * Wrong matter type * Names from another client matter * Wrong address * Inconsistency with the estate planning packet 6. Bilingual Document Inconsistencies Some packets are English only. Others include English and Spanish. Aegis should detect when the English version and Spanish version do not match on key information, including: * Names * Roles * Fiduciaries * Beneficiaries * Property references * Distribution terms * Client instructions * Engagement terms 7. Missing or Placeholder Information The system should flag blanks, placeholders, bracketed text, template remnants, or incomplete fields. Examples: * “[Client Name]” * “[Spouse Name]” * “INSERT ADDRESS” * Wrong template language * Unfilled notary or witness sections * Inconsistent dates 8. Contradictions Between Drafted Documents The system should compare all documents in the packet against each other. Example: * Trust says Child A receives 100% * Will or memo says Child A and Child B share equally * Power of attorney names Wife as agent * Intake says Child is supposed to be agent 9. Contradictions Between Drafts and Client Communications The system should compare drafted documents against intake forms, emails, meeting transcripts, Teams chats, and phone calls. Example: * Client said in the meeting that daughter Maria should be successor trustee * Draft names son Carlos as successor trustee * Engagement letter references a joint trust, but documents are drafted as individual trusts Required Data Sources The system should be designed to ingest, search, and compare against: 1. Estate planning packet documents 2. Engagement letters 3. Intake forms 4. Zoom meeting transcripts and cloud meeting recordings 5. Zoom phone call recordings and transcripts 6. Outlook emails 7. Microsoft Teams meeting transcripts/recordings 8. Microsoft Teams chat messages 9. Firm templates and premade forms 10. Optional: prior versions of the same documents Preferred Technical Architecture We are looking for someone who can recommend and build the right architecture, but we expect the project may involve: * Amazon Bedrock * Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases * Amazon Bedrock Agents, if appropriate * Amazon S3 * Amazon Transcribe or equivalent transcription pipeline * Amazon Textract or equivalent OCR/document extraction * Vector database such as OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora pgvector, or another appropriate tool * AWS Lambda or containerized ingestion services * IAM least-privilege permissions * KMS encryption * CloudWatch logging * Microsoft Graph API * Outlook / Microsoft 365 integration * Microsoft Teams integration * Zoom API * Secure document parsing for Word and PDF files We are open to the developer’s recommendations, but the solution must be secure, maintainable, and appropriate for confidential law firm data. Required Features 1. Estate Planning Packet Upload and Review Staff should be able to upload or select an estate planning packet and ask Aegis to review it. The review should include: * Client identity check * Spouse identity check * Children and family member check * Fiduciary role check * Beneficiary check * Distribution check * Address/property check * Document consistency check * Engagement letter consistency check * Bilingual consistency check, when applicable * Cross-client name contamination check * Placeholder/template-remnant check 2. Source-of-Truth Extraction Aegis should extract a “source-of-truth” profile for the matter from the available materials. This should include: * Client name * Spouse name * Children * Other family members * Trustees * Personal representatives * Agents under power of attorney * Health care surrogates * Beneficiaries * Distribution instructions * Property addresses * Special instructions * Scope of engagement * Important meeting statements or client instructions 3. Document Comparison Aegis should compare the source-of-truth profile against the drafted documents and identify discrepancies. 4. Structured Error Report The output should be a structured report with: * Issue number * Severity level: Critical / High / Medium / Low * Document name * Page number or section, if available * Problem found * Why it may be wrong * Correct source information * Source citation * Suggested correction * Whether attorney review is required Example output: Issue 3 — Critical Document: Revocable Trust Section: Article 4, Beneficiaries Problem: The draft lists “Maria Lopez” as the client’s child. Concern: Intake form identifies Maria Lopez as the client’s spouse, not child. Source: Intake form dated [date]; Zoom transcript dated [date]. Suggested correction: Review beneficiary section and confirm intended child/beneficiary. Attorney review required: Yes. 5. No Unsupported Legal Conclusions Aegis should not make final legal judgments. It should identify possible drafting issues for attorney or staff review. The system should use language such as: * “Potential inconsistency” * “Possible drafting error” * “Needs attorney review” * “Source does not support this name/role” * “Insufficient information to confirm” 6. Source Citations Required Every flagged issue should cite the source material used to identify the potential problem. Sources may include: * Intake form * Meeting transcript * Email * Teams chat * Zoom call transcript * Engagement letter * Prior version of document * Firm-approved template 7. Cross-Matter Protection This is very important. Aegis should help detect if a name, address, family member, fiduciary, or other client-specific information from a different matter appears in the current packet. The system should be designed to reduce the risk of false positives but should flag suspicious names that are unsupported by the current matter’s source materials. 8. Confidentiality and Security This is a law firm system. Confidentiality is essential. The solution must include: * Encryption at rest and in transit * Secure AWS architecture * IAM least-privilege access * Matter-level access control * Audit logs * No unnecessary third-party data sharing * No use of client data to train public models * Ability to delete/exclude data * Secure handling of transcripts, recordings, emails, and legal documents * Documentation of all data flows Bonus Feature: Document Drafting Bonus points if the developer can also build a system where Aegis can help draft estate planning documents using our premade forms/templates. This should be treated as a possible Phase 2 or optional feature. The drafting system would ideally: * Use our approved templates/forms * Pull client information from intake forms and source materials * Populate the correct fields * Avoid cross-client contamination * Produce a draft for human review * Generate a change log or source map showing where each inserted fact came from * Never finalize documents without attorney/staff approval We are especially interested in developers who understand the difference between: 1. AI-assisted document drafting, and 2. AI quality-control review of attorney-drafted documents. The first priority is quality-control review. Drafting is a bonus. Deliverables The selected freelancer or agency should deliver: 1. Architecture proposal 2. Security design 3. Data-flow diagram 4. AWS Bedrock environment setup 5. Document ingestion and parsing system 6. Intake/source-of-truth extraction system 7. Estate planning packet review workflow 8. Engagement letter review workflow 9. Bilingual review approach 10. Cross-matter contamination detection 11. Structured issue-report generator 12. Source citation system 13. Test dataset using dummy or sanitized matters 14. Pilot implementation 15. Documentation 16. Staff training/handoff 17. Cost estimate and AWS cost-control recommendations 18. Optional Phase 2 proposal for document drafting from templates Suggested Milestones Milestone 1: Discovery and Architecture * Review our current document workflow * Identify data sources * Confirm AWS architecture * Confirm Microsoft 365, Outlook, Teams, Zoom, and document storage requirements * Design the matter-level security model * Provide implementation plan and estimated AWS cost range Milestone 2: Proof of Concept * Use dummy or sanitized estate planning packets * Ingest sample intake forms, transcripts, emails, and documents * Demonstrate detection of wrong names, repeated names, and cross-matter contamination * Produce a structured review report with citations Milestone 3: Estate Planning Packet Review MVP * Build the main document review workflow * Review 120 to 200 page packets * Extract key names, roles, fiduciaries, beneficiaries, and distribution terms * Compare drafted documents against source materials * Generate issue reports Milestone 4: Engagement Letter Review * Add review of engagement letters * Compare engagement letters against intake, client communications, and estate planning packet * Flag wrong names, wrong matter scope, wrong parties, and cross-client contamination Milestone 5: Communications and Meeting Data Integration * Add ingestion from Outlook emails * Add ingestion from Microsoft Teams meetings and chats * Add ingestion from Zoom meeting transcripts and Zoom phone calls * Match communications to the correct matter Milestone 6: Security, Testing, and Pilot Rollout * Implement permissions * Implement audit logs * Test with a limited number of real or sanitized matters * Tune issue severity levels * Reduce false positives * Improve report quality Milestone 7: Production Rollout and Handoff * Deploy production version * Train staff * Provide documentation * Provide maintenance plan * Recommend future improvements Optional Milestone 8: AI-Assisted Drafting From Firm Templates * Analyze firm templates * Build controlled document-generation workflow * Populate templates using source materials * Generate drafts for attorney/staff review * Produce source map for inserted facts * Add safeguards against cross-client contamination Required Experience Please apply only if you have experience with several of the following: * Amazon Bedrock * Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases * Retrieval-Augmented Generation * Legal document AI review * Long-document review * AWS S3 * AWS IAM * AWS KMS * AWS Lambda * Vector databases * Amazon Transcribe or audio transcription * Amazon Textract or OCR/document Amazon Bedrock

Upwork Job Post: Build “Project Michael Jordan” — AWS Bedrock Legal Knowledge Assistant Accessible Through Microsoft Teams Job Title AWS Bedrock / Microsoft Teams AI Engineer Needed to Build Secure Legal Knowledge Assistant for Law Firm Overview We are a Florida law firm looking to build an internal AI knowledge assistant named Project Michael Jordan, also called Mike. The purpose of Mike is to serve as an internal, secure, matter-aware AI assistant for our staff. Instead of interrupting teammates to ask about case status, documents, client communications, meetings, or next steps, staff should be able to ask Mike directly inside Microsoft Teams. This is not a generic chatbot project. This is a secure legal knowledge-management system that must retrieve answers from our actual firm data, cite the sources used, respect matter-level permissions, and avoid hallucinations. Since December 15, our firm has processed approximately 265 client matters, and we need a scalable system that can help staff quickly answer questions about current and recent matters. Main Goal Build an internal AI assistant that can answer staff questions such as: * What is the latest status of this client matter? * What did the client say in the last Zoom or Microsoft Teams meeting? * What was promised to the client? * Which documents have been drafted? * Which documents are still missing? * What was the last email communication? * What is the next known task or follow-up? * Are there inconsistencies between the intake, meeting transcript, emails, and drafted documents? * What source supports this answer? Mike must answer using retrieval from firm data, not unsupported guesses. Required Data Sources The system should be designed to ingest, index, and retrieve from the following sources: 1. Microsoft Teams meetings * Recordings * Transcripts * Meeting chats * Meeting files * Attendance/participants when available * The developer must help determine the proper Microsoft 365, Teams Admin, and Microsoft Graph API setup required to access recordings and transcripts lawfully and securely. 2. Microsoft Teams interface * Mike must be accessible as a Microsoft Teams bot/app. * Staff should be able to ask questions directly from Teams. * The bot should support matter-specific questions and general firm knowledge questions. 3. Zoom meetings * Recorded client meetings * Zoom transcripts * Zoom cloud recordings * Meeting metadata and participants when available 4. Recorded client calls * Zoom Phone or other call-recording sources, depending on our current system * Audio recordings should be transcribed and indexed 5. Emails * Microsoft 365 / Outlook / Exchange and/or Gmail, depending on final firm setup * Emails must be associated with the correct client matter when possible * Attachments should also be considered 6. Drafted documents * Word documents * PDFs * Estate planning documents * Probate documents * Deeds * Letters * Court filings * Intake forms * Internal notes * Documents stored in Microsoft OneDrive, SharePoint, Google Drive, local folders, or another document-management source Preferred Architecture We are leaning toward an AWS-based solution using: * Amazon Bedrock * Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases * Amazon S3 * Amazon Transcribe or equivalent transcription pipeline * Amazon Textract or equivalent OCR/document extraction where needed * Amazon OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora pgvector, or another appropriate vector store * AWS Lambda or containerized ingestion services * IAM least-privilege access controls * KMS encryption * CloudWatch logging * Microsoft Graph API * Microsoft Teams Bot Framework / Teams SDK * Zoom API * Optional: Amazon Q Business if appropriate for connectors and enterprise search We are open to recommendations, but the solution must be secure, maintainable, and appropriate for a law firm handling confidential client information. Key Functional Requirements 1. Matter-Aware Retrieval The system must organize data by client matter. A matter may include emails, documents, Zoom recordings, Teams meetings, call transcripts, and internal notes. The assistant should be able to understand that multiple files, recordings, and emails relate to the same client matter. 2. Microsoft Teams Bot Access Mike must be available to staff inside Microsoft Teams. Example interaction: Staff member asks in Teams: Mike, what is the latest status of the Hernandez probate matter? Mike responds with: * Brief answer * Current known status * Last relevant communication * Pending tasks * Source citations * Date/time of source material * Confidence level * Warning if the answer is incomplete or unsupported 3. Source Citations Required Every answer must cite the underlying source, such as: * Email from date/time * Teams meeting transcript from date/time * Zoom meeting transcript from date/time * Document name and date * Call transcript date/time Mike should not provide unsupported factual answers. 4. No Hallucinations The assistant must be designed to say: I do not have enough information in the available sources to answer that. This is preferable to guessing. 5. Security and Confidentiality This is a law firm system. Confidentiality is essential. The solution must include: * Encryption at rest and in transit * AWS IAM least-privilege design * Matter-level access control * User-level access control * Audit logs of user questions and system responses * No use of client data to train public models * No unnecessary copying of data to third-party systems * Secure handling of transcripts, recordings, emails, and legal documents * Ability to delete or exclude data from the index * Documentation of all data flows 6. Teams Meeting Recording and Transcription We want Mike to capture and learn from Microsoft Teams meetings. The developer should help us determine and implement the proper approach for: * Teams recording policies * Teams transcription policies * Microsoft Graph permissions * Application access policies * Post-meeting retrieval of recordings and transcripts * Notifications when new recordings/transcripts are available * Storage of Teams recordings/transcripts in AWS or another secure location * Matching Teams meeting data to the correct client matter Important: this must be done in a compliant, transparent, and secure manner. We understand that admin policies, Microsoft 365 licensing, user consent, and Graph permissions may affect the final implementation. 7. Zoom Meeting and Call Ingestion The developer should design ingestion for: * Zoom cloud recordings * Zoom meeting transcripts * Zoom Phone/client call recordings, if available * Speaker identification where possible * Matter matching based on client name, email, phone number, meeting title, or manually assigned matter ID 8. Email and Document Ingestion The system should ingest emails and documents and associate them with matters. Preferred features: * Connectors for Microsoft 365 / Outlook / Exchange * Connectors for Gmail if needed * Connectors for OneDrive / SharePoint / Google Drive if needed * Attachment extraction * PDF and Word document parsing * OCR for scanned documents * Matter-level tagging * Deduplication * Metadata extraction 9. Admin Dashboard or Configuration Layer We need a way to manage: * Matter IDs * Client aliases * Staff access * Excluded documents * Data-source connections * Failed ingestion jobs * Recent updates * System health * Cost monitoring * Audit logs This does not need to be beautiful at first, but it must be usable. Deliverables The selected freelancer or agency should deliver: 1. Architecture proposal 2. Security design 3. Data-flow diagram 4. AWS environment setup 5. Microsoft Teams bot/app setup 6. Microsoft Graph integration plan 7. Teams recording/transcript ingestion pipeline 8. Zoom ingestion pipeline 9. Email/document ingestion pipeline 10. Matter-mapping system 11. RAG retrieval and response pipeline 12. Source citation system 13. Test dataset / pilot implementation 14. Staff-facing Microsoft Teams assistant 15. Admin documentation 16. Deployment documentation 17. Cost estimate and cost-monitoring recommendations 18. Handoff/training session Suggested Milestones Milestone 1: Discovery and Architecture * Review our current systems * Confirm data sources * Confirm Microsoft 365 / Teams / Zoom / AWS requirements * Propose final architecture * Identify licensing/API limitations * Provide implementation plan and estimated AWS cost range Milestone 2: Proof of Concept * Build a small working prototype using sample or sanitized matter data * Ingest limited documents, emails, and transcripts * Demonstrate citation-based answers * Demonstrate “I don’t know” behavior when sources are insufficient Milestone 3: Microsoft Teams Bot MVP * Deploy Mike inside Microsoft Teams * Allow staff to ask questions through Teams * Connect Teams bot to the AWS Bedrock retrieval system * Include source citations in responses Milestone 4: Meeting Recording and Transcript Pipeline * Implement Microsoft Teams transcript/recording retrieval where permitted * Implement Zoom transcript/recording retrieval * Store and index meeting/call data securely * Match meeting/call data to client matters Milestone 5: Matter-Level Security and Pilot Rollout * Implement user permissions * Implement audit logs * Test with a limited number of real matters * Validate answers against source documents * Improve prompts, retrieval, and matter matching Milestone 6: Full Rollout and Handoff * Deploy to production * Train staff * Provide documentation * Provide maintenance plan * Provide recommendations for future improvements Required Experience Please apply only if you have experience with several of the following: * Amazon Bedrock * Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases * Retrieval-Augmented Generation * AWS Lambda * AWS S3 * AWS IAM * AWS KMS * Amazon OpenSearch Serverless or vector databases * Amazon Transcribe * Amazon Textract or OCR/document extraction * Microsoft Graph API * Microsoft Teams bot development * Microsoft 365 admin/API permissions * Teams recordings and transcripts * Zoom API * Secure enterprise data ingestion * Legal, healthcare, finance, or other confidential-data environments Strong Preference We prefer someone who has already built: * Internal enterprise AI assistants * RAG systems with citations * Microsoft Teams bots * AWS Bedrock applications * Microsoft Graph integrations * Meeting transcript ingestion systems * Secure document-search systems for professional-services firms Important Rules * Do not propose a generic ChatGPT wrapper. * Do not propose uploading confidential legal data to random third-party tools. * Do not propose fine-tuning on client data unless you clearly explain why it is necessary. We believe RAG is likely the better approach. * Do not apply if you cannot explain security and permissions. * Do not apply if you cannot build source-cited answers. * Do not apply if you cannot work with Microsoft Teams and Microsoft Graph. * Do not apply with a generic AI-generated cover letter. Screening Questions Please answer the following in your application: 1. Have you built an AWS Bedrock RAG system before? Please describe it. 2. Have you built a Microsoft Teams bot before? Please describe it. 3. Have you worked with Microsoft Graph APIs for Teams meetings, transcripts, recordings, chats, or files? 4. How would you retrieve and ingest Teams meeting transcripts and recordings? 5. How would you organize data by client matter? 6. How would you prevent the assistant from hallucinating? 7. How would you make the assistant cite its sources? 8. How would you handle matter-level permissions so staff only access appropriate information? 9. What AWS services would you use for this project and why? 10. How would you estimate and control AWS usage costs? 11. What are the biggest risks or limitations you see in this project? 12. Please include links or descriptions of similar projects you have completed. GHL Sync: Key metrics (like DSCR or NOI) are pushed back to GHL Custom Fields via API v2, and the completed Excel file is saved to the client's profile.
